Context

This section explains and publishes project context under .claude/context/.

What Context Is

Section titled “What Context Is”

Context in this repository is canonical guidance intended for both agents and humans:

  • navigable (cheap routing before deep reads)
  • stable (IDs and paths should not churn)
  • token-efficient (small entrypoints, selective loading)
  • machine-checkable (deterministic structure and validation)

It is a primary mechanism for consistent instruction-following across tools and sessions.

How To Use This Section

Section titled “How To Use This Section”
  • Overview (this page): conceptual explanation plus maintenance workflow.
  • Reference: generated, human-readable pages for XML files under .claude/context.

Reference pages are generated from source during docs prebuild and CI deploy.

Source of Truth

Section titled “Source of Truth”

The source of truth remains .claude/context in the repository. Generated pages are a documentation view, not an authoring surface.

Principles

Section titled “Principles”

Small entrypoints first

Section titled “Small entrypoints first”

Prefer short entrypoints that link to deeper content instead of one giant file.

Selective loading

Section titled “Selective loading”

Indexes should include short descriptions so agents can decide what to load next.

Stable IDs and paths

Section titled “Stable IDs and paths”

Use stable identifiers so specs and plans can cite standards reliably.

Keep files small

Section titled “Keep files small”

As a rule of thumb, keep context files to ~80 lines or fewer and split when they grow.

Coding Standards Packs

Section titled “Coding Standards Packs”

Coding standards are ID-addressable so specs and plans can cite them directly:

  • General: Y### (guidelines), Z### (anti-patterns)
  • Python: PY###, PZ###
  • Java: JY###, JZ###

Entrypoint (opens in new tab):

  • .claude/context/coding-standards.md

Example citation format:

Maintaining Context

Section titled “Maintaining Context”

Use /update-context (or $update-context) when updating .claude/context/ while preserving navigability and machine-checkability.

Canonical sources (open in new tab):

  • .claude/commands/update-context.md
  • .claude/skills/update-context/SKILL.md
  • .claude/context/README.md
  • .claude/context/AGENTS.md

After context changes:

If coding standards XML changed:

Do And Don’t

Section titled “Do And Don’t”
  • Do treat .claude/context/ as canonical and link to it instead of duplicating.
  • Do cite standards by ID in spec.md and plan.md.
  • Don’t rename stable IDs casually (breaks traceability).
  • Don’t add large, unindexed context files (split and index instead).

index catalog

Catalog Overview

Section titled “Catalog Overview”
  • Guidelines
    • Y1xx — Code Organization & Modularity
      • Y10x — Principles — SRP, boundaries, cohesion, and coupling.
      • Y11x — Structure and Patterns — Module layout, constants, composition patterns.
    • Y2xx — Design Patterns
      • Y23x — Architectural — System-level patterns and data access.
      • Y22x — Behavioral — Algorithms and object interaction patterns.
      • Y20x — Creational — Object creation and lifecycle choices.
      • Y21x — Structural — Composition, wrappers, interface adaptation.
    • Y3xx — Documentation & Readability
      • Y31x — Maintenance and Readability — Cleanup, TODOs, constants, assertions.
      • Y30x — Naming and Comments — Names, booleans, and comment intent.
    • Y4xx — Testing
      • Y41x — Quality — Isolation, speed, helpers, TDD, coverage.
      • Y40x — Structure — Naming, AAA, fixtures, parameterization.
  • Anti-Patterns
    • Z1xx — Coupling Issues
      • Z11x — Change Smells — Divergent change, parallel inheritance, temporary fields.
      • Z10x — Coupling Smells — Tight coupling, message chains, data clumps.
    • Z2xx — Poor Abstractions
      • Z20x — Poor Abstractions — Abstraction mistakes and design smells.
    • Z3xx — Testing Anti-patterns
      • Z30x — Testing Anti-patterns — Common testing pitfalls.
    • Z4xx — Miscellaneous
      • Z40x — Miscellaneous — Other general anti-patterns.

Guidelines

Section titled “Guidelines”

Normative rules to follow.

Y1xx — Code Organization & Modularity

Section titled “Y1xx — Code Organization & Modularity”

Structure, modularity, and boundaries.

Y10x — Principles

Section titled “Y10x — Principles”

SRP, boundaries, cohesion, and coupling.

RuleSummarySource
Y100-SINGLE-RESPONSIBILITYEach class/module should have one reason to change (SRP). SRP, boundaries, cohesion, and coupling.y1xx/principles.xml
Y101-INTERFACE-SEGREGATIONMany small, specific interfaces are better than one large one. SRP, boundaries, cohesion, and coupling.y1xx/principles.xml
Y102-SEPARATION-OF-CONCERNSSeparate business logic, data access, presentation, and infrastructure. SRP, boundaries, cohesion, and coupling.y1xx/principles.xml
Y103-DEPENDENCY-INJECTIONPass dependencies as parameters, do not create them internally. SRP, boundaries, cohesion, and coupling.y1xx/principles.xml
Y104-DEPENDENCY-INVERSIONDepend on abstractions (interfaces), not concrete classes. SRP, boundaries, cohesion, and coupling.y1xx/principles.xml
Y105-OPEN-CLOSEDOpen for extension, closed for modification. SRP, boundaries, cohesion, and coupling.y1xx/principles.xml
Y106-MODULE-STRUCTUREOrganize modules by feature/domain, not by type. SRP, boundaries, cohesion, and coupling.y1xx/principles.xml
Y107-COHESIONKeep related functionality together. High cohesion within modules. SRP, boundaries, cohesion, and coupling.y1xx/principles.xml
Y108-COUPLINGMinimize dependencies between modules. Loose coupling preferred. SRP, boundaries, cohesion, and coupling.y1xx/principles.xml
Y109-FLAT-OVER-NESTEDPrefer flat module structure over deep nesting. SRP, boundaries, cohesion, and coupling.y1xx/principles.xml

Y11x — Structure and Patterns

Section titled “Y11x — Structure and Patterns”

Module layout, constants, composition patterns.

RuleSummarySource
Y110-CONSTANTS-MODULEGroup many constants in a dedicated module. Module layout, constants, composition patterns.y1xx/structure-and-patterns.xml
Y111-UTILS-ANTIPATTERNAvoid catch-all utils modules. Use specific names. Module layout, constants, composition patterns.y1xx/structure-and-patterns.xml
Y112-FACADE-PATTERNProvide a simple interface to a complex subsystem. Module layout, constants, composition patterns.y1xx/structure-and-patterns.xml
Y113-COMPOSITION-OVER-INHERITANCEPrefer composition (has-a) over inheritance (is-a). Module layout, constants, composition patterns.y1xx/structure-and-patterns.xml
Y114-ADAPTER-PATTERNConvert the interface of one class to another clients expect. Module layout, constants, composition patterns.y1xx/structure-and-patterns.xml

Y2xx — Design Patterns

Section titled “Y2xx — Design Patterns”

Pattern catalog by category.

Y23x — Architectural

Section titled “Y23x — Architectural”

System-level patterns and data access.

RuleSummarySource
Y230-NULL-OBJECT-PATTERNUse object with do-nothing behavior instead of None checks. System-level patterns and data access.y2xx/architectural.xml
Y231-REGISTRY-PATTERNGlobal registry of objects by key. System-level patterns and data access.y2xx/architectural.xml
Y232-DEPENDENCY-CONTAINERIoC container managing object creation and dependencies. System-level patterns and data access.y2xx/architectural.xml
Y233-REPOSITORY-PATTERNMediates between domain and data mapping layers. System-level patterns and data access.y2xx/architectural.xml
Y234-SPECIFICATION-PATTERNBusiness rules as composable objects. System-level patterns and data access.y2xx/architectural.xml
Y235-EVENT-SOURCINGStore state changes as sequence of events. System-level patterns and data access.y2xx/architectural.xml
Y236-PLUGIN-ARCHITECTURELoad and execute code dynamically. System-level patterns and data access.y2xx/architectural.xml
Y237-ACTIVE-RECORDObject wraps database row with data access methods. System-level patterns and data access.y2xx/architectural.xml
Y238-MONOSTATE-PATTERNAll instances share state (alternative to Singleton). System-level patterns and data access.y2xx/architectural.xml
Y239-DOUBLE-DISPATCHDynamic dispatch based on two objects’ types. System-level patterns and data access.y2xx/architectural.xml

Y22x — Behavioral

Section titled “Y22x — Behavioral”

Algorithms and object interaction patterns.

RuleSummarySource
Y220-STRATEGY-PATTERNDefine family of algorithms, make them interchangeable. Algorithms and object interaction patterns.y2xx/behavioral.xml
Y221-OBSERVER-PATTERNDefine subscription mechanism to notify multiple objects of events. Algorithms and object interaction patterns.y2xx/behavioral.xml
Y222-COMMAND-PATTERNEncapsulate request as object, enabling queuing, logging, undo. Algorithms and object interaction patterns.y2xx/behavioral.xml
Y223-TEMPLATE-METHODDefine algorithm skeleton, let subclasses override steps. Algorithms and object interaction patterns.y2xx/behavioral.xml
Y224-ITERATOR-PATTERNAccess elements sequentially without exposing representation. Algorithms and object interaction patterns.y2xx/behavioral.xml
Y225-STATE-PATTERNChange behavior when internal state changes. Algorithms and object interaction patterns.y2xx/behavioral.xml
Y226-CHAIN-OF-RESPONSIBILITYPass request along chain until handled. Algorithms and object interaction patterns.y2xx/behavioral.xml
Y227-MEDIATOR-PATTERNEncapsulate how objects interact. Algorithms and object interaction patterns.y2xx/behavioral.xml
Y228-MEMENTO-PATTERNCapture and restore object state. Algorithms and object interaction patterns.y2xx/behavioral.xml
Y229-VISITOR-PATTERNSeparate algorithm from object structure. Algorithms and object interaction patterns.y2xx/behavioral.xml

Y20x — Creational

Section titled “Y20x — Creational”

Object creation and lifecycle choices.

RuleSummarySource
Y200-FACTORY-METHODDefine interface for creating objects, let subclasses decide which class to instantiate. Object creation and lifecycle choices.y2xx/creational.xml
Y201-ABSTRACT-FACTORYCreate families of related objects without specifying concrete classes. Object creation and lifecycle choices.y2xx/creational.xml
Y202-BUILDER-PATTERNConstruct complex objects step by step. Object creation and lifecycle choices.y2xx/creational.xml
Y203-SINGLETON-PATTERNEnsure class has only one instance. Use sparingly. Object creation and lifecycle choices.y2xx/creational.xml
Y204-PROTOTYPE-PATTERNClone objects instead of creating new ones. Object creation and lifecycle choices.y2xx/creational.xml
Y205-OBJECT-POOLReuse expensive objects from pool. Object creation and lifecycle choices.y2xx/creational.xml
Y206-LAZY-INITIALIZATIONDefer expensive object creation until needed. Object creation and lifecycle choices.y2xx/creational.xml

Y21x — Structural

Section titled “Y21x — Structural”

Composition, wrappers, interface adaptation.

RuleSummarySource
Y210-ADAPTER-PATTERNConvert one interface to another clients expect. Composition, wrappers, interface adaptation.y2xx/structural.xml
Y211-FACADE-PATTERNUnified interface to set of interfaces in subsystem. Composition, wrappers, interface adaptation.y2xx/structural.xml
Y212-DECORATOR-PATTERNAdd behavior to objects dynamically. Composition, wrappers, interface adaptation.y2xx/structural.xml
Y213-PROXY-PATTERNProvide placeholder/surrogate for another object. Composition, wrappers, interface adaptation.y2xx/structural.xml
Y214-COMPOSITE-PATTERNCompose objects into tree structures. Composition, wrappers, interface adaptation.y2xx/structural.xml
Y215-FLYWEIGHT-PATTERNShare common state to support large numbers of objects. Composition, wrappers, interface adaptation.y2xx/structural.xml
Y216-BRIDGE-PATTERNDecouple abstraction from implementation. Composition, wrappers, interface adaptation.y2xx/structural.xml

Y3xx — Documentation & Readability

Section titled “Y3xx — Documentation & Readability”

Naming, comments, and clarity.

Y31x — Maintenance and Readability

Section titled “Y31x — Maintenance and Readability”

Cleanup, TODOs, constants, assertions.

RuleSummarySource
Y310-REMOVE-DEAD-CODEDelete commented-out code. Use version control history. Cleanup, TODOs, constants, assertions.y3xx/maintenance.xml
Y311-TODO-COMMENTSUse TODO: description for temporary markers. Cleanup, TODOs, constants, assertions.y3xx/maintenance.xml
Y312-CONSTANTS-NAMINGConstants use UPPER_CASE_WITH_UNDERSCORES. Cleanup, TODOs, constants, assertions.y3xx/maintenance.xml
Y313-SELF-DOCUMENTINGWrite code that explains itself. Reduce need for comments. Cleanup, TODOs, constants, assertions.y3xx/maintenance.xml
Y314-MAGIC-NUMBERSExtract magic numbers to named constants. Cleanup, TODOs, constants, assertions.y3xx/maintenance.xml
Y315-ASSERTION-MESSAGESInclude messages in assertions explaining what failed. Cleanup, TODOs, constants, assertions.y3xx/maintenance.xml

Y30x — Naming and Comments

Section titled “Y30x — Naming and Comments”

Names, booleans, and comment intent.

RuleSummarySource
Y300-EXPRESSIVE-NAMESUse clear, descriptive names. Avoid unnecessary abbreviations. Names, booleans, and comment intent.y3xx/naming.xml
Y301-VERB-FUNCTIONSFunction names start with verbs. Names, booleans, and comment intent.y3xx/naming.xml
Y302-NOUN-CLASSESClass names are nouns. Names, booleans, and comment intent.y3xx/naming.xml
Y303-BOOL-NAMINGBoolean variables and functions use is_, has_, can_. Names, booleans, and comment intent.y3xx/naming.xml
Y304-COMMENT-WHY-NOT-WHATComments explain why, not what (code shows what). Names, booleans, and comment intent.y3xx/naming.xml

Y4xx — Testing

Section titled “Y4xx — Testing”

Testing structure and quality.

Y41x — Quality

Section titled “Y41x — Quality”

Isolation, speed, helpers, TDD, coverage.

RuleSummarySource
Y410-ISOLATIONTests should be independent and runnable in any order. Isolation, speed, helpers, TDD, coverage.y4xx/quality.xml
Y411-FAST-TESTSUnit tests should be fast (<1s each). Isolation, speed, helpers, TDD, coverage.y4xx/quality.xml
Y412-TEST-HELPERSExtract common test utilities to helper functions or fixtures. Isolation, speed, helpers, TDD, coverage.y4xx/quality.xml
Y413-DOCTESTUse doc-tests where supported to validate examples. Isolation, speed, helpers, TDD, coverage.y4xx/quality.xml
Y414-PROPERTY-TESTINGConsider property-based testing for complex logic. Isolation, speed, helpers, TDD, coverage.y4xx/quality.xml
Y415-TDDWrite tests before implementation (Test-Driven Development). Isolation, speed, helpers, TDD, coverage.y4xx/quality.xml
Y416-TEST-COVERAGEAim for high coverage of business logic (80%+). Isolation, speed, helpers, TDD, coverage.y4xx/quality.xml

Y40x — Structure

Section titled “Y40x — Structure”

Naming, AAA, fixtures, parameterization.

RuleSummarySource
Y400-TEST-NAMINGTest names describe what they test and expected behavior. Naming, AAA, fixtures, parameterization.y4xx/structure.xml
Y401-AAA-PATTERNTests follow Arrange-Act-Assert structure. Naming, AAA, fixtures, parameterization.y4xx/structure.xml
Y402-ONE-ASSERT-CONCEPTEach test verifies one concept (multiple asserts OK if related). Naming, AAA, fixtures, parameterization.y4xx/structure.xml
Y403-FIXTURESUse fixtures or shared setup helpers for reusable test data. Naming, AAA, fixtures, parameterization.y4xx/structure.xml
Y404-PARAMETRIZEUse data-driven tests for multiple inputs and outputs. Naming, AAA, fixtures, parameterization.y4xx/structure.xml
Y405-MOCKINGMock external dependencies (APIs, databases), not internal logic. Naming, AAA, fixtures, parameterization.y4xx/structure.xml
Y406-INTEGRATION-TESTSTest full workflows, not just units. Naming, AAA, fixtures, parameterization.y4xx/structure.xml
Y407-TEST-DATAUse realistic test data and include edge cases. Naming, AAA, fixtures, parameterization.y4xx/structure.xml

Anti-Patterns

Section titled “Anti-Patterns”

Patterns to avoid.

Z1xx — Coupling Issues

Section titled “Z1xx — Coupling Issues”

Coupling smells and change pain.

Z11x — Change Smells

Section titled “Z11x — Change Smells”

Divergent change, parallel inheritance, temporary fields.

RuleSummarySource
Z110-DIVERGENT-CHANGEClass changes for multiple different reasons. Divergent change, parallel inheritance, temporary fields.z1xx/change-smells.xml
Z111-REFUSED-BEQUESTSubclass doesn’t use parent’s methods. Divergent change, parallel inheritance, temporary fields.z1xx/change-smells.xml
Z112-PARALLEL-INHERITANCEAdding subclass in one hierarchy requires adding in another. Divergent change, parallel inheritance, temporary fields.z1xx/change-smells.xml
Z113-SPECULATIVE-GENERALITYCode for future features that aren’t needed yet. Divergent change, parallel inheritance, temporary fields.z1xx/change-smells.xml
Z114-TEMPORARY-FIELDInstance variable only used in some methods. Divergent change, parallel inheritance, temporary fields.z1xx/change-smells.xml

Z10x — Coupling Smells

Section titled “Z10x — Coupling Smells”

Tight coupling, message chains, data clumps.

RuleSummarySource
Z100-TIGHT-COUPLINGClasses/modules directly depend on concrete implementations. Tight coupling, message chains, data clumps.z1xx/coupling-smells.xml
Z101-CIRCULAR-DEPSModule A imports B, B imports A. Tight coupling, message chains, data clumps.z1xx/coupling-smells.xml
Z102-GOD-OBJECTSOne class does everything. Tight coupling, message chains, data clumps.z1xx/coupling-smells.xml
Z103-FEATURE-ENVYMethod uses another class’s data more than its own. Tight coupling, message chains, data clumps.z1xx/coupling-smells.xml
Z104-INAPPROPRIATE-INTIMACYClasses know too much about each other’s internals. Tight coupling, message chains, data clumps.z1xx/coupling-smells.xml
Z105-MESSAGE-CHAINSLong chains like a.b.c.d.e(). Tight coupling, message chains, data clumps.z1xx/coupling-smells.xml
Z106-MIDDLE-MANClass just delegates to another class. Tight coupling, message chains, data clumps.z1xx/coupling-smells.xml
Z107-DATA-CLUMPSSame group of parameters passed together everywhere. Tight coupling, message chains, data clumps.z1xx/coupling-smells.xml
Z108-PRIMITIVE-OBSESSIONUsing primitives instead of small objects. Tight coupling, message chains, data clumps.z1xx/coupling-smells.xml
Z109-SHOTGUN-SURGERYSingle change requires modifying many classes. Tight coupling, message chains, data clumps.z1xx/coupling-smells.xml

Z2xx — Poor Abstractions

Section titled “Z2xx — Poor Abstractions”

Abstraction mistakes and design smells.

Z20x — Poor Abstractions

Section titled “Z20x — Poor Abstractions”

Abstraction mistakes and design smells.

RuleSummarySource
Z200-LEAKY-ABSTRACTIONImplementation details leak through interface. Abstraction mistakes and design smells.z2xx/z2xx-poor-abstractions.xml
Z201-WRONG-ABSTRACTION-LEVELMixing high and low-level concepts. Abstraction mistakes and design smells.z2xx/z2xx-poor-abstractions.xml
Z202-PREMATURE-ABSTRACTIONAbstracting before patterns emerge. Abstraction mistakes and design smells.z2xx/z2xx-poor-abstractions.xml
Z203-MISSING-ABSTRACTIONDuplicated code that should be abstracted. Abstraction mistakes and design smells.z2xx/z2xx-poor-abstractions.xml
Z204-INCOMPLETE-ABSTRACTIONAbstraction doesn’t cover all cases. Abstraction mistakes and design smells.z2xx/z2xx-poor-abstractions.xml
Z205-BOOLEAN-PARAMETERSFunction with boolean flag changing behavior. Abstraction mistakes and design smells.z2xx/z2xx-poor-abstractions.xml
Z206-LONG-PARAMETER-LISTFunction with many parameters (>5). Abstraction mistakes and design smells.z2xx/z2xx-poor-abstractions.xml
Z207-OUTPUT-PARAMETERModifying argument to return value. Abstraction mistakes and design smells.z2xx/z2xx-poor-abstractions.xml
Z208-FLAG-ARGUMENTBoolean that completely changes function behavior. Abstraction mistakes and design smells.z2xx/z2xx-poor-abstractions.xml
Z209-TRAIN-WRECKLong method chains a.b().c().d().e(). Abstraction mistakes and design smells.z2xx/z2xx-poor-abstractions.xml

Z3xx — Testing Anti-patterns

Section titled “Z3xx — Testing Anti-patterns”

Common testing pitfalls.

Z30x — Testing Anti-patterns

Section titled “Z30x — Testing Anti-patterns”

Common testing pitfalls.

RuleSummarySource
Z300-TESTING-IMPLEMENTATIONTests know too much about implementation details. Common testing pitfalls.z3xx/z3xx-testing-antipatterns.xml
Z301-BRITTLE-TESTSTests break on unrelated changes. Common testing pitfalls.z3xx/z3xx-testing-antipatterns.xml
Z302-TEST-INTERDEPENDENCETests depend on each other, must run in order. Common testing pitfalls.z3xx/z3xx-testing-antipatterns.xml
Z303-OVER-MOCKINGMocking everything including internal logic. Common testing pitfalls.z3xx/z3xx-testing-antipatterns.xml
Z304-MISSING-EDGE-CASESOnly happy path tested. Common testing pitfalls.z3xx/z3xx-testing-antipatterns.xml
Z305-SLOW-TESTSUnit tests take minutes to run. Common testing pitfalls.z3xx/z3xx-testing-antipatterns.xml
Z306-TESTING-PRIVATE-METHODSTests call private methods directly. Common testing pitfalls.z3xx/z3xx-testing-antipatterns.xml
Z307-ASSERTION-ROULETTEMultiple unrelated asserts, unclear which failed. Common testing pitfalls.z3xx/z3xx-testing-antipatterns.xml
Z308-TEST-CODE-IN-PRODUCTIONTest utilities, fixtures in production code. Common testing pitfalls.z3xx/z3xx-testing-antipatterns.xml
Z309-IGNORING-FAILURESCommented-out failing tests. Common testing pitfalls.z3xx/z3xx-testing-antipatterns.xml

Z4xx — Miscellaneous

Section titled “Z4xx — Miscellaneous”

Other general anti-patterns.

Z40x — Miscellaneous

Section titled “Z40x — Miscellaneous”

Other general anti-patterns.

RuleSummarySource
Z400-MAGIC-NUMBERSUnexplained constants in code. Other general anti-patterns.z4xx/z4xx-miscellaneous.xml
Z401-DEEP-NESTINGCode nested 5+ levels deep. Other general anti-patterns.z4xx/z4xx-miscellaneous.xml
Z402-LONG-FUNCTIONSFunctions > 50 lines. Other general anti-patterns.z4xx/z4xx-miscellaneous.xml
Z403-INCONSISTENT-NAMINGSimilar things named differently. Other general anti-patterns.z4xx/z4xx-miscellaneous.xml
Z404-COMMENTED-OUT-CODEOld code left commented out. Other general anti-patterns.z4xx/z4xx-miscellaneous.xml

View raw XML source

index

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
PrinciplesSRP, boundaries, cohesion, and coupling../principles.xml
Structure and PatternsModule layout, constants, composition patterns../structure-and-patterns.xml

View raw XML source

Principles

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
Y100-SINGLE-RESPONSIBILITYEach class/module should have one reason to change (SRP).
Y101-INTERFACE-SEGREGATIONMany small, specific interfaces are better than one large one.
Y102-SEPARATION-OF-CONCERNSSeparate business logic, data access, presentation, and infrastructure.
Y103-DEPENDENCY-INJECTIONPass dependencies as parameters, do not create them internally.
Y104-DEPENDENCY-INVERSIONDepend on abstractions (interfaces), not concrete classes.
Y105-OPEN-CLOSEDOpen for extension, closed for modification.
Y106-MODULE-STRUCTUREOrganize modules by feature/domain, not by type.
Y107-COHESIONKeep related functionality together.
Y108-COUPLINGMinimize dependencies between modules.
Y109-FLAT-OVER-NESTEDPrefer flat module structure over deep nesting.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

Y100-SINGLE-RESPONSIBILITY

Section titled “Y100-SINGLE-RESPONSIBILITY”

Each class/module should have one reason to change (SRP).

  • Good Example: Separate Parser (reads data) from Validator (checks data) from Transformer (converts data).

  • Related: Y102-SEPARATION-OF-CONCERNS, Z102-GOD-OBJECTS

Back to Rule Catalog

Y101-INTERFACE-SEGREGATION

Section titled “Y101-INTERFACE-SEGREGATION”

Many small, specific interfaces are better than one large one.

  • Good Example: Readable, Writable, Seekable interfaces instead of one File.

  • Related: Y103-DEPENDENCY-INJECTION

Back to Rule Catalog

Y102-SEPARATION-OF-CONCERNS

Section titled “Y102-SEPARATION-OF-CONCERNS”

Separate business logic, data access, presentation, and infrastructure.

  • Good Example: TransactionRepository (data), TransactionService (logic), TransactionAPI (presentation).

  • Related: Y100-SINGLE-RESPONSIBILITY

Back to Rule Catalog

Y103-DEPENDENCY-INJECTION

Section titled “Y103-DEPENDENCY-INJECTION”

Pass dependencies as parameters, do not create them internally.

  • Good Example: DataProcessor(parser, producer) rather than a constructor that instantiates its own dependencies.

  • Related: Y101-INTERFACE-SEGREGATION, Y220-STRATEGY-PATTERN

Back to Rule Catalog

Y104-DEPENDENCY-INVERSION

Section titled “Y104-DEPENDENCY-INVERSION”

Depend on abstractions (interfaces), not concrete classes.

  • Good Example: process(parser: Parser) rather than process(parser: CsvParser).

  • Related: Y101-INTERFACE-SEGREGATION

Back to Rule Catalog

Y105-OPEN-CLOSED

Section titled “Y105-OPEN-CLOSED”

Open for extension, closed for modification.

  • Good Example: Extend behavior with new implementations instead of editing existing code.

  • Related: Y220-STRATEGY-PATTERN, Y223-TEMPLATE-METHOD

Back to Rule Catalog

Y106-MODULE-STRUCTURE

Section titled “Y106-MODULE-STRUCTURE”

Organize modules by feature/domain, not by type.

  • Good Example: users/auth/, users/profile/ not models/, parsers/.

  • Related: Y100-SINGLE-RESPONSIBILITY

Back to Rule Catalog

Y107-COHESION

Section titled “Y107-COHESION”

Keep related functionality together. High cohesion within modules.

  • Good Example: All CSV parsing logic lives in one module, not scattered across multiple files.

  • Related: Y100-SINGLE-RESPONSIBILITY

Back to Rule Catalog

Y108-COUPLING

Section titled “Y108-COUPLING”

Minimize dependencies between modules. Loose coupling preferred.

  • Good Example: Use events or callbacks instead of direct cross-module calls.

  • Related: Y103-DEPENDENCY-INJECTION, Z100-TIGHT-COUPLING

Back to Rule Catalog

Y109-FLAT-OVER-NESTED

Section titled “Y109-FLAT-OVER-NESTED”

Prefer flat module structure over deep nesting.

  • Good Example: user_auth and user_profile modules instead of 5+ levels of folders.

Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

Structure and Patterns

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
Y110-CONSTANTS-MODULEGroup many constants in a dedicated module.
Y111-UTILS-ANTIPATTERNAvoid catch-all utils modules.
Y112-FACADE-PATTERNProvide a simple interface to a complex subsystem.
Y113-COMPOSITION-OVER-INHERITANCEPrefer composition (has-a) over inheritance (is-a).
Y114-ADAPTER-PATTERNConvert the interface of one class to another clients expect.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

Y110-CONSTANTS-MODULE

Section titled “Y110-CONSTANTS-MODULE”

Group many constants in a dedicated module.

  • Good Example: config module with DATE_FORMAT, MAX_RETRIES, etc.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y111-UTILS-ANTIPATTERN

Section titled “Y111-UTILS-ANTIPATTERN”

Avoid catch-all utils modules. Use specific names.

  • Good Example: date_helpers not utils.

  • Related: Y100-SINGLE-RESPONSIBILITY, Z201-WRONG-ABSTRACTION-LEVEL

Back to Rule Catalog

Y112-FACADE-PATTERN

Section titled “Y112-FACADE-PATTERN”

Provide a simple interface to a complex subsystem.

  • Good Example: BankingAPI hides parsing, validation, and conversion details.

  • Related: Y211-FACADE-PATTERN

Back to Rule Catalog

Y113-COMPOSITION-OVER-INHERITANCE

Section titled “Y113-COMPOSITION-OVER-INHERITANCE”

Prefer composition (has-a) over inheritance (is-a).

  • Good Example: DataProcessor has a Parser and Producer rather than inheriting.

  • Related: Y103-DEPENDENCY-INJECTION

Back to Rule Catalog

Y114-ADAPTER-PATTERN

Section titled “Y114-ADAPTER-PATTERN”

Convert the interface of one class to another clients expect.

  • Good Example: OrderAdapter wraps a third-party order object to match an internal OrderSource interface.

  • Related: Y210-ADAPTER-PATTERN

Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

Architectural

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
Y230-NULL-OBJECT-PATTERNUse object with do-nothing behavior instead of None checks.
Y231-REGISTRY-PATTERNGlobal registry of objects by key.
Y232-DEPENDENCY-CONTAINERIoC container managing object creation and dependencies.
Y233-REPOSITORY-PATTERNMediates between domain and data mapping layers.
Y234-SPECIFICATION-PATTERNBusiness rules as composable objects.
Y235-EVENT-SOURCINGStore state changes as sequence of events.
Y236-PLUGIN-ARCHITECTURELoad and execute code dynamically.
Y237-ACTIVE-RECORDObject wraps database row with data access methods.
Y238-MONOSTATE-PATTERNAll instances share state (alternative to Singleton).
Y239-DOUBLE-DISPATCHDynamic dispatch based on two objects’ types.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

Y230-NULL-OBJECT-PATTERN

Section titled “Y230-NULL-OBJECT-PATTERN”

Use object with do-nothing behavior instead of None checks.

  • Exceptions: Modern Python uses Optional[T] and explicit None checks instead.
  • Good Example: NullLogger that implements Logger but does nothing.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y231-REGISTRY-PATTERN

Section titled “Y231-REGISTRY-PATTERN”

Global registry of objects by key.

  • Good Example: Parser registry: register_parser("csv", CsvParser).

Back to Rule Catalog

Y232-DEPENDENCY-CONTAINER

Section titled “Y232-DEPENDENCY-CONTAINER”

IoC container managing object creation and dependencies.

  • Good Example: Simple dict-based container for dependency injection.

  • Related: Y103-DEPENDENCY-INJECTION

Back to Rule Catalog

Y233-REPOSITORY-PATTERN

Section titled “Y233-REPOSITORY-PATTERN”

Mediates between domain and data mapping layers.

  • Good Example: TransactionRepository with .find_by_id(), .save() methods.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y234-SPECIFICATION-PATTERN

Section titled “Y234-SPECIFICATION-PATTERN”

Business rules as composable objects.

  • Good Example: AmountGreaterThan(100) &amp; CategoryEquals("Food") specifications.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y235-EVENT-SOURCING

Section titled “Y235-EVENT-SOURCING”

Store state changes as sequence of events.

  • Good Example: Transaction audit log with all state transitions.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y236-PLUGIN-ARCHITECTURE

Section titled “Y236-PLUGIN-ARCHITECTURE”

Load and execute code dynamically.

  • Good Example: Parser plugins discovered via entry points.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y237-ACTIVE-RECORD

Section titled “Y237-ACTIVE-RECORD”

Object wraps database row with data access methods.

  • Exceptions: Prefer repository pattern for better testability (Y233).
  • Good Example: transaction.save(), transaction.delete().

Back to Rule Catalog

Y238-MONOSTATE-PATTERN

Section titled “Y238-MONOSTATE-PATTERN”

All instances share state (alternative to Singleton).

  • Good Example: Class where all instances access shared class variables.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y239-DOUBLE-DISPATCH

Section titled “Y239-DOUBLE-DISPATCH”

Dynamic dispatch based on two objects’ types.

  • Good Example: Visitor pattern implementation.

Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

Behavioral

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
Y220-STRATEGY-PATTERNDefine family of algorithms, make them interchangeable.
Y221-OBSERVER-PATTERNDefine subscription mechanism to notify multiple objects of events.
Y222-COMMAND-PATTERNEncapsulate request as object, enabling queuing, logging, undo.
Y223-TEMPLATE-METHODDefine algorithm skeleton, let subclasses override steps.
Y224-ITERATOR-PATTERNAccess elements sequentially without exposing representation.
Y225-STATE-PATTERNChange behavior when internal state changes.
Y226-CHAIN-OF-RESPONSIBILITYPass request along chain until handled.
Y227-MEDIATOR-PATTERNEncapsulate how objects interact.
Y228-MEMENTO-PATTERNCapture and restore object state.
Y229-VISITOR-PATTERNSeparate algorithm from object structure.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

Y220-STRATEGY-PATTERN

Section titled “Y220-STRATEGY-PATTERN”

Define family of algorithms, make them interchangeable.

  • Good Example: Different Parser implementations, selected at runtime via DataProcessor(parser=...).

  • Related: Y103-DEPENDENCY-INJECTION

Back to Rule Catalog

Y221-OBSERVER-PATTERN

Section titled “Y221-OBSERVER-PATTERN”

Define subscription mechanism to notify multiple objects of events.

  • Good Example: Event system where TransactionProcessor notifies listeners of completed transactions.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y222-COMMAND-PATTERN

Section titled “Y222-COMMAND-PATTERN”

Encapsulate request as object, enabling queuing, logging, undo.

  • Good Example: ParseCommand, ValidateCommand, TransformCommand with .execute() method.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y223-TEMPLATE-METHOD

Section titled “Y223-TEMPLATE-METHOD”

Define algorithm skeleton, let subclasses override steps.

  • Good Example: ABC Parser with parse() that calls abstract parse_line(), validate_line().

  • Related: Y105-OPEN-CLOSED

Back to Rule Catalog

Y224-ITERATOR-PATTERN

Section titled “Y224-ITERATOR-PATTERN”

Access elements sequentially without exposing representation.

  • Good Example: Parser yields transactions one at a time with yield.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y225-STATE-PATTERN

Section titled “Y225-STATE-PATTERN”

Change behavior when internal state changes.

  • Good Example: Connection with states: Connected, Disconnected, Connecting.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y226-CHAIN-OF-RESPONSIBILITY

Section titled “Y226-CHAIN-OF-RESPONSIBILITY”

Pass request along chain until handled.

  • Good Example: Validation pipeline where each validator checks and passes to next.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y227-MEDIATOR-PATTERN

Section titled “Y227-MEDIATOR-PATTERN”

Encapsulate how objects interact.

  • Good Example: EventBus mediates communication between publishers and subscribers.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y228-MEMENTO-PATTERN

Section titled “Y228-MEMENTO-PATTERN”

Capture and restore object state.

  • Good Example: Transaction history with undo/redo using snapshots.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y229-VISITOR-PATTERN

Section titled “Y229-VISITOR-PATTERN”

Separate algorithm from object structure.

  • Good Example: TransactionVisitor with visit_debit(), visit_credit() methods.

Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

Creational

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
Y200-FACTORY-METHODDefine interface for creating objects, let subclasses decide which class to instantiate.
Y201-ABSTRACT-FACTORYCreate families of related objects without specifying concrete classes.
Y202-BUILDER-PATTERNConstruct complex objects step by step.
Y203-SINGLETON-PATTERNEnsure class has only one instance.
Y204-PROTOTYPE-PATTERNClone objects instead of creating new ones.
Y205-OBJECT-POOLReuse expensive objects from pool.
Y206-LAZY-INITIALIZATIONDefer expensive object creation until needed.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

Y200-FACTORY-METHOD

Section titled “Y200-FACTORY-METHOD”

Define interface for creating objects, let subclasses decide which class to instantiate.

  • Good Example: Parser.create(format="csv") returns CsvParser or JsonParser.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y201-ABSTRACT-FACTORY

Section titled “Y201-ABSTRACT-FACTORY”

Create families of related objects without specifying concrete classes.

  • Good Example: BankingFactory creates matching parser, validator, and producer for a bank type.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y202-BUILDER-PATTERN

Section titled “Y202-BUILDER-PATTERN”

Construct complex objects step by step.

  • Good Example: TransactionBuilder().with_date(...).with_amount(...).build().

Back to Rule Catalog

Y203-SINGLETON-PATTERN

Section titled “Y203-SINGLETON-PATTERN”

Ensure class has only one instance. Use sparingly.

  • Exceptions: Often better as module-level instance or dependency injection.
  • Good Example: Database connection pool, configuration manager.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y204-PROTOTYPE-PATTERN

Section titled “Y204-PROTOTYPE-PATTERN”

Clone objects instead of creating new ones.

  • Good Example: copy.copy() or copy.deepcopy() for cloning.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y205-OBJECT-POOL

Section titled “Y205-OBJECT-POOL”

Reuse expensive objects from pool.

  • Good Example: Database connection pool.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y206-LAZY-INITIALIZATION

Section titled “Y206-LAZY-INITIALIZATION”

Defer expensive object creation until needed.

  • Tool Support: functools.cached_property.
  • Good Example: @cached_property for expensive computed attributes.

Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

index

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
CreationalObject creation and lifecycle choices../creational.xml
StructuralComposition, wrappers, interface adaptation../structural.xml
BehavioralAlgorithms and object interaction patterns../behavioral.xml
ArchitecturalSystem-level patterns and data access../architectural.xml

View raw XML source

Structural

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
Y210-ADAPTER-PATTERNConvert one interface to another clients expect.
Y211-FACADE-PATTERNUnified interface to set of interfaces in subsystem.
Y212-DECORATOR-PATTERNAdd behavior to objects dynamically.
Y213-PROXY-PATTERNProvide placeholder/surrogate for another object.
Y214-COMPOSITE-PATTERNCompose objects into tree structures.
Y215-FLYWEIGHT-PATTERNShare common state to support large numbers of objects.
Y216-BRIDGE-PATTERNDecouple abstraction from implementation.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

Y210-ADAPTER-PATTERN

Section titled “Y210-ADAPTER-PATTERN”

Convert one interface to another clients expect.

  • Good Example: Adapt pandas DataFrame to Sequence[Transaction] protocol.

  • Related: Y114-ADAPTER-PATTERN

Back to Rule Catalog

Y211-FACADE-PATTERN

Section titled “Y211-FACADE-PATTERN”

Unified interface to set of interfaces in subsystem.

  • Good Example: BankingAPI hides complexity of parsing, validation, conversion.

  • Related: Y112-FACADE-PATTERN

Back to Rule Catalog

Y212-DECORATOR-PATTERN

Section titled “Y212-DECORATOR-PATTERN”

Add behavior to objects dynamically.

  • Good Example: @log_calls, @retry, @cache decorators that wrap functions.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y213-PROXY-PATTERN

Section titled “Y213-PROXY-PATTERN”

Provide placeholder/surrogate for another object.

  • Good Example: Lazy-loading proxy that loads data only when first accessed.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y214-COMPOSITE-PATTERN

Section titled “Y214-COMPOSITE-PATTERN”

Compose objects into tree structures.

  • Good Example: CompositeValidator contains multiple validators, validates all.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y215-FLYWEIGHT-PATTERN

Section titled “Y215-FLYWEIGHT-PATTERN”

Share common state to support large numbers of objects.

  • Exceptions: Python does this automatically for small ints/strings.
  • Good Example: String interning, cached immutable objects.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y216-BRIDGE-PATTERN

Section titled “Y216-BRIDGE-PATTERN”

Decouple abstraction from implementation.

  • Good Example: Separate Parser (abstraction) from LineReader (implementation).

Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

index

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
Naming and CommentsNames, booleans, and comment intent../naming.xml
Maintenance and ReadabilityCleanup, TODOs, constants, assertions../maintenance.xml

View raw XML source

Maintenance and Readability

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
Y310-REMOVE-DEAD-CODEDelete commented-out code.
Y311-TODO-COMMENTSUse TODO: description for temporary markers.
Y312-CONSTANTS-NAMINGConstants use UPPER_CASE_WITH_UNDERSCORES.
Y313-SELF-DOCUMENTINGWrite code that explains itself.
Y314-MAGIC-NUMBERSExtract magic numbers to named constants.
Y315-ASSERTION-MESSAGESInclude messages in assertions explaining what failed.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

Y310-REMOVE-DEAD-CODE

Section titled “Y310-REMOVE-DEAD-CODE”

Delete commented-out code. Use version control history.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y311-TODO-COMMENTS

Section titled “Y311-TODO-COMMENTS”

Use TODO: description for temporary markers.

  • Exceptions: Reference issue numbers when available.
  • Good Example: // TODO: Handle edge case when amount is zero.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y312-CONSTANTS-NAMING

Section titled “Y312-CONSTANTS-NAMING”

Constants use UPPER_CASE_WITH_UNDERSCORES.

  • Good Example: MAX_RETRIES, DEFAULT_TIMEOUT.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y313-SELF-DOCUMENTING

Section titled “Y313-SELF-DOCUMENTING”

Write code that explains itself. Reduce need for comments.

  • Good Example: is_valid_transaction() better than check() with a comment.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y314-MAGIC-NUMBERS

Section titled “Y314-MAGIC-NUMBERS”

Extract magic numbers to named constants.

  • Good Example: TAX_RATE = 0.19; tax = amount * TAX_RATE not tax = amount * 0.19.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y315-ASSERTION-MESSAGES

Section titled “Y315-ASSERTION-MESSAGES”

Include messages in assertions explaining what failed.

  • Good Example: assert amount &gt; 0, "amount must be positive".

Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

Naming and Comments

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
Y300-EXPRESSIVE-NAMESUse clear, descriptive names.
Y301-VERB-FUNCTIONSFunction names start with verbs.
Y302-NOUN-CLASSESClass names are nouns.
Y303-BOOL-NAMINGBoolean variables and functions use is_, has_, can_.
Y304-COMMENT-WHY-NOT-WHATComments explain why, not what (code shows what).

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

Y300-EXPRESSIVE-NAMES

Section titled “Y300-EXPRESSIVE-NAMES”

Use clear, descriptive names. Avoid unnecessary abbreviations.

  • Exceptions: Common abbreviations are OK when widely understood.
  • Good Example: transaction_parser not txn_prs.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y301-VERB-FUNCTIONS

Section titled “Y301-VERB-FUNCTIONS”

Function names start with verbs.

  • Good Example: parse_transaction() not transaction().

Back to Rule Catalog

Y302-NOUN-CLASSES

Section titled “Y302-NOUN-CLASSES”

Class names are nouns.

  • Good Example: TransactionParser not ParseTransaction.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y303-BOOL-NAMING

Section titled “Y303-BOOL-NAMING”

Boolean variables and functions use is_, has_, can_.

  • Good Example: is_valid, has_errors, can_parse.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y304-COMMENT-WHY-NOT-WHAT

Section titled “Y304-COMMENT-WHY-NOT-WHAT”

Comments explain why, not what (code shows what).

  • Good Example: # Use Decimal to avoid float precision errors.

Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

index

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
StructureNaming, AAA, fixtures, parameterization../structure.xml
QualityIsolation, speed, helpers, TDD, coverage../quality.xml

View raw XML source

Quality

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
Y410-ISOLATIONTests should be independent and runnable in any order.
Y411-FAST-TESTSUnit tests should be fast (<1s each).
Y412-TEST-HELPERSExtract common test utilities to helper functions or fixtures.
Y413-DOCTESTUse doc-tests where supported to validate examples.
Y414-PROPERTY-TESTINGConsider property-based testing for complex logic.
Y415-TDDWrite tests before implementation (Test-Driven Development).
Y416-TEST-COVERAGEAim for high coverage of business logic (80%+).

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

Y410-ISOLATION

Section titled “Y410-ISOLATION”

Tests should be independent and runnable in any order.

  • Good Example: No shared mutable state across tests.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y411-FAST-TESTS

Section titled “Y411-FAST-TESTS”

Unit tests should be fast (<1s each).

  • Good Example: Stub slow network or disk operations.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y412-TEST-HELPERS

Section titled “Y412-TEST-HELPERS”

Extract common test utilities to helper functions or fixtures.

  • Good Example: make_transaction() factory for tests.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y413-DOCTEST

Section titled “Y413-DOCTEST”

Use doc-tests where supported to validate examples.

  • Good Example: Run examples in documentation as tests.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y414-PROPERTY-TESTING

Section titled “Y414-PROPERTY-TESTING”

Consider property-based testing for complex logic.

  • Good Example: Generate random inputs to validate invariants.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y415-TDD

Section titled “Y415-TDD”

Write tests before implementation (Test-Driven Development).

  • Good Example: Red (failing test) → Green (make it pass) → Refactor.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y416-TEST-COVERAGE

Section titled “Y416-TEST-COVERAGE”

Aim for high coverage of business logic (80%+).

  • Exceptions: Prioritize critical paths over trivial getters/setters. Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

Structure

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
Y400-TEST-NAMINGTest names describe what they test and expected behavior.
Y401-AAA-PATTERNTests follow Arrange-Act-Assert structure.
Y402-ONE-ASSERT-CONCEPTEach test verifies one concept (multiple asserts OK if related).
Y403-FIXTURESUse fixtures or shared setup helpers for reusable test data.
Y404-PARAMETRIZEUse data-driven tests for multiple inputs and outputs.
Y405-MOCKINGMock external dependencies (APIs, databases), not internal logic.
Y406-INTEGRATION-TESTSTest full workflows, not just units.
Y407-TEST-DATAUse realistic test data and include edge cases.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

Y400-TEST-NAMING

Section titled “Y400-TEST-NAMING”

Test names describe what they test and expected behavior.

  • Good Example: test_parse_invalid_date_raises_error.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y401-AAA-PATTERN

Section titled “Y401-AAA-PATTERN”

Tests follow Arrange-Act-Assert structure.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”

Back to Rule Catalog

Y402-ONE-ASSERT-CONCEPT

Section titled “Y402-ONE-ASSERT-CONCEPT”

Each test verifies one concept (multiple asserts OK if related).

  • Good Example: Test amount parsing separately from date parsing.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y403-FIXTURES

Section titled “Y403-FIXTURES”

Use fixtures or shared setup helpers for reusable test data.

  • Good Example: sample_transaction() helper used across tests.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y404-PARAMETRIZE

Section titled “Y404-PARAMETRIZE”

Use data-driven tests for multiple inputs and outputs.

  • Good Example: A table of inputs and expected results in one test.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y405-MOCKING

Section titled “Y405-MOCKING”

Mock external dependencies (APIs, databases), not internal logic.

  • Good Example: Mock HTTP calls in unit tests; keep business logic real.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y406-INTEGRATION-TESTS

Section titled “Y406-INTEGRATION-TESTS”

Test full workflows, not just units.

  • Good Example: Parser + validator + persistence end-to-end.

Back to Rule Catalog

Y407-TEST-DATA

Section titled “Y407-TEST-DATA”

Use realistic test data and include edge cases.

  • Good Example: Empty strings, max values, negative numbers, invalid formats.

Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

Change Smells

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
Z110-DIVERGENT-CHANGEClass changes for multiple different reasons.
Z111-REFUSED-BEQUESTSubclass doesn’t use parent’s methods.
Z112-PARALLEL-INHERITANCEAdding subclass in one hierarchy requires adding in another.
Z113-SPECULATIVE-GENERALITYCode for future features that aren’t needed yet.
Z114-TEMPORARY-FIELDInstance variable only used in some methods.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

Z110-DIVERGENT-CHANGE

Section titled “Z110-DIVERGENT-CHANGE”

Class changes for multiple different reasons.

  • Better: Y100-SINGLE-RESPONSIBILITY
  • Why This Is Problematic: Violates SRP, hard to maintain. Back to Rule Catalog

Z111-REFUSED-BEQUEST

Section titled “Z111-REFUSED-BEQUEST”

Subclass doesn’t use parent’s methods.

  • Better: Y113-COMPOSITION-OVER-INHERITANCE
  • Why This Is Problematic: Wrong inheritance hierarchy. Back to Rule Catalog

Z112-PARALLEL-INHERITANCE

Section titled “Z112-PARALLEL-INHERITANCE”

Adding subclass in one hierarchy requires adding in another.

  • Better: Y113-COMPOSITION-OVER-INHERITANCE
  • Why This Is Problematic: Maintenance burden, duplication. Back to Rule Catalog

Z113-SPECULATIVE-GENERALITY

Section titled “Z113-SPECULATIVE-GENERALITY”

Code for future features that aren’t needed yet.

  • Better: Build what you need now.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Complexity without benefit, YAGNI violation. Back to Rule Catalog

Z114-TEMPORARY-FIELD

Section titled “Z114-TEMPORARY-FIELD”

Instance variable only used in some methods.

  • Better: Pass as parameter or extract class.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Confusing, suggests wrong abstraction. Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

Coupling Smells

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
Z100-TIGHT-COUPLINGClasses/modules directly depend on concrete implementations.
Z101-CIRCULAR-DEPSModule A imports B, B imports A.
Z102-GOD-OBJECTSOne class does everything.
Z103-FEATURE-ENVYMethod uses another class’s data more than its own.
Z104-INAPPROPRIATE-INTIMACYClasses know too much about each other’s internals.
Z105-MESSAGE-CHAINSLong chains like a.b.c.d.e().
Z106-MIDDLE-MANClass just delegates to another class.
Z107-DATA-CLUMPSSame group of parameters passed together everywhere.
Z108-PRIMITIVE-OBSESSIONUsing primitives instead of small objects.
Z109-SHOTGUN-SURGERYSingle change requires modifying many classes.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

Z100-TIGHT-COUPLING

Section titled “Z100-TIGHT-COUPLING”

Classes/modules directly depend on concrete implementations.

  • Better: Y103-DEPENDENCY-INJECTION Y104-DEPENDENCY-INVERSION
  • Why This Is Problematic: Changes ripple through system, hard to test, inflexible. Back to Rule Catalog

Z101-CIRCULAR-DEPS

Section titled “Z101-CIRCULAR-DEPS”

Module A imports B, B imports A.

  • Better: Break dependency cycles with clear interfaces and layering.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Import errors, unclear dependencies, fragile. Back to Rule Catalog

Z102-GOD-OBJECTS

Section titled “Z102-GOD-OBJECTS”

One class does everything.

  • Better: Y100-SINGLE-RESPONSIBILITY Y102-SEPARATION-OF-CONCERNS
  • Why This Is Problematic: Hard to understand, test, maintain. Violates SRP. Back to Rule Catalog

Z103-FEATURE-ENVY

Section titled “Z103-FEATURE-ENVY”

Method uses another class’s data more than its own.

  • Better: Move method to the class it envies.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Wrong responsibility placement, tight coupling. Back to Rule Catalog

Z104-INAPPROPRIATE-INTIMACY

Section titled “Z104-INAPPROPRIATE-INTIMACY”

Classes know too much about each other’s internals.

  • Better: Y101-INTERFACE-SEGREGATION Y112-FACADE-PATTERN
  • Why This Is Problematic: Breaks encapsulation, fragile to changes. Back to Rule Catalog

Z105-MESSAGE-CHAINS

Section titled “Z105-MESSAGE-CHAINS”

Long chains like a.b.c.d.e().

  • Better: Add methods to intermediate objects.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Fragile, violates Law of Demeter. Back to Rule Catalog

Z106-MIDDLE-MAN

Section titled “Z106-MIDDLE-MAN”

Class just delegates to another class.

  • Better: Remove middle man or give it real responsibility.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Unnecessary indirection. Back to Rule Catalog

Z107-DATA-CLUMPS

Section titled “Z107-DATA-CLUMPS”

Same group of parameters passed together everywhere.

  • Better: Introduce a parameter object or value object.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Should be an object. Back to Rule Catalog

Z108-PRIMITIVE-OBSESSION

Section titled “Z108-PRIMITIVE-OBSESSION”

Using primitives instead of small objects.

  • Better: Introduce value objects or enums instead of primitives.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Loses type safety, business meaning. Back to Rule Catalog

Z109-SHOTGUN-SURGERY

Section titled “Z109-SHOTGUN-SURGERY”

Single change requires modifying many classes.

  • Better: Y100-SINGLE-RESPONSIBILITY Y107-COHESION
  • Why This Is Problematic: Error-prone, indicates poor design. Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

index

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
Coupling SmellsTight coupling, message chains, data clumps../coupling-smells.xml
Change SmellsDivergent change, parallel inheritance, temporary fields../change-smells.xml

View raw XML source

index

Source

Section titled “Source”

Related context files:

  • z2xx-poor-abstractions.xml

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
Poor AbstractionsAbstraction mistakes and design smells../z2xx-poor-abstractions.xml

View raw XML source

Poor Abstractions

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
Z200-LEAKY-ABSTRACTIONImplementation details leak through interface.
Z201-WRONG-ABSTRACTION-LEVELMixing high and low-level concepts.
Z202-PREMATURE-ABSTRACTIONAbstracting before patterns emerge.
Z203-MISSING-ABSTRACTIONDuplicated code that should be abstracted.
Z204-INCOMPLETE-ABSTRACTIONAbstraction doesn’t cover all cases.
Z205-BOOLEAN-PARAMETERSFunction with boolean flag changing behavior.
Z206-LONG-PARAMETER-LISTFunction with many parameters (>5).
Z207-OUTPUT-PARAMETERModifying argument to return value.
Z208-FLAG-ARGUMENTBoolean that completely changes function behavior.
Z209-TRAIN-WRECKLong method chains a.b().c().d().e().

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

Z200-LEAKY-ABSTRACTION

Section titled “Z200-LEAKY-ABSTRACTION”

Implementation details leak through interface.

  • Better: Hide implementation, stable interface.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Users coupled to internals, defeats abstraction. Back to Rule Catalog

Z201-WRONG-ABSTRACTION-LEVEL

Section titled “Z201-WRONG-ABSTRACTION-LEVEL”

Mixing high and low-level concepts.

  • Better: Y102-SEPARATION-OF-CONCERNS
  • Why This Is Problematic: Hard to understand, change, test.
  • Bad Example: Business logic mixed with SQL queries.

Back to Rule Catalog

Z202-PREMATURE-ABSTRACTION

Section titled “Z202-PREMATURE-ABSTRACTION”

Abstracting before patterns emerge.

  • Better: Wait for 3rd use case (Rule of Three).
  • Why This Is Problematic: Wrong abstraction is worse than duplication. Back to Rule Catalog

Z203-MISSING-ABSTRACTION

Section titled “Z203-MISSING-ABSTRACTION”

Duplicated code that should be abstracted.

  • Better: Extract common code when pattern clear.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Maintenance burden, inconsistency. Back to Rule Catalog

Z204-INCOMPLETE-ABSTRACTION

Section titled “Z204-INCOMPLETE-ABSTRACTION”

Abstraction doesn’t cover all cases.

  • Better: Rethink abstraction or accept limitations.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Special cases leak out. Back to Rule Catalog

Z205-BOOLEAN-PARAMETERS

Section titled “Z205-BOOLEAN-PARAMETERS”

Function with boolean flag changing behavior.

  • Better: Two functions or Y220-STRATEGY-PATTERN.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Two functions in one, unclear at call site.
  • Bad Example: def process(data, strict=True)

Back to Rule Catalog

Z206-LONG-PARAMETER-LIST

Section titled “Z206-LONG-PARAMETER-LIST”

Function with many parameters (>5).

  • Better: Use a parameter object or options struct.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Hard to remember, use, test. Back to Rule Catalog

Z207-OUTPUT-PARAMETER

Section titled “Z207-OUTPUT-PARAMETER”

Modifying argument to return value.

  • Better: Return value explicitly.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Unclear, violates functional style.
  • Bad Example: def compute(input, output_list) modifies output_list

Back to Rule Catalog

Z208-FLAG-ARGUMENT

Section titled “Z208-FLAG-ARGUMENT”

Boolean that completely changes function behavior.

  • Better: Split into two functions.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Really two different functions. Back to Rule Catalog

Z209-TRAIN-WRECK

Section titled “Z209-TRAIN-WRECK”

Long method chains a.b().c().d().e().

  • Better: Y112-FACADE-PATTERN or fewer levels.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Tight coupling, fragile. Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

index

Source

Section titled “Source”

Related context files:

  • z3xx-testing-antipatterns.xml

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
Testing Anti-patternsCommon testing pitfalls../z3xx-testing-antipatterns.xml

View raw XML source

Testing Anti-patterns

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
Z300-TESTING-IMPLEMENTATIONTests know too much about implementation details.
Z301-BRITTLE-TESTSTests break on unrelated changes.
Z302-TEST-INTERDEPENDENCETests depend on each other, must run in order.
Z303-OVER-MOCKINGMocking everything including internal logic.
Z304-MISSING-EDGE-CASESOnly happy path tested.
Z305-SLOW-TESTSUnit tests take minutes to run.
Z306-TESTING-PRIVATE-METHODSTests call private methods directly.
Z307-ASSERTION-ROULETTEMultiple unrelated asserts, unclear which failed.
Z308-TEST-CODE-IN-PRODUCTIONTest utilities, fixtures in production code.
Z309-IGNORING-FAILURESCommented-out failing tests.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

Z300-TESTING-IMPLEMENTATION

Section titled “Z300-TESTING-IMPLEMENTATION”

Tests know too much about implementation details.

  • Better: Test behavior, not implementation.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Brittle, refactoring breaks tests. Back to Rule Catalog

Z301-BRITTLE-TESTS

Section titled “Z301-BRITTLE-TESTS”

Tests break on unrelated changes.

  • Better: Y410-ISOLATION, test public interface.
  • Why This Is Problematic: False negatives, maintenance burden. Back to Rule Catalog

Z302-TEST-INTERDEPENDENCE

Section titled “Z302-TEST-INTERDEPENDENCE”

Tests depend on each other, must run in order.

  • Better: Y410-ISOLATION
  • Why This Is Problematic: Can’t run individually, hard to debug. Back to Rule Catalog

Z303-OVER-MOCKING

Section titled “Z303-OVER-MOCKING”

Mocking everything including internal logic.

  • Better: Y405-MOCKING only external dependencies.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Tests don’t test real code. Back to Rule Catalog

Z304-MISSING-EDGE-CASES

Section titled “Z304-MISSING-EDGE-CASES”

Only happy path tested.

  • Better: Y407-TEST-DATA with edge cases.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Bugs in error handling. Back to Rule Catalog

Z305-SLOW-TESTS

Section titled “Z305-SLOW-TESTS”

Unit tests take minutes to run.

  • Better: Y411-FAST-TESTS, use mocks.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Developers don’t run them. Back to Rule Catalog

Z306-TESTING-PRIVATE-METHODS

Section titled “Z306-TESTING-PRIVATE-METHODS”

Tests call private methods directly.

  • Better: Test through public interface.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Couples tests to implementation. Back to Rule Catalog

Z307-ASSERTION-ROULETTE

Section titled “Z307-ASSERTION-ROULETTE”

Multiple unrelated asserts, unclear which failed.

  • Better: Y402-ONE-ASSERT-CONCEPT
  • Why This Is Problematic: Hard to debug failures. Back to Rule Catalog

Z308-TEST-CODE-IN-PRODUCTION

Section titled “Z308-TEST-CODE-IN-PRODUCTION”

Test utilities, fixtures in production code.

  • Better: Separate test code.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Bloat, security risk. Back to Rule Catalog

Z309-IGNORING-FAILURES

Section titled “Z309-IGNORING-FAILURES”

Commented-out failing tests.

  • Better: Fix or remove tests.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Technical debt, false confidence. Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

index

Source

Section titled “Source”

Related context files:

  • z4xx-miscellaneous.xml

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
MiscellaneousOther general anti-patterns../z4xx-miscellaneous.xml

View raw XML source

Miscellaneous

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
Z400-MAGIC-NUMBERSUnexplained constants in code.
Z401-DEEP-NESTINGCode nested 5+ levels deep.
Z402-LONG-FUNCTIONSFunctions > 50 lines.
Z403-INCONSISTENT-NAMINGSimilar things named differently.
Z404-COMMENTED-OUT-CODEOld code left commented out.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

Z400-MAGIC-NUMBERS

Section titled “Z400-MAGIC-NUMBERS”

Unexplained constants in code.

  • Better: Y314-MAGIC-NUMBERS with named constants.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Unclear meaning, hard to maintain.
  • Bad Example: if age &gt; 65: what does 65 mean?

Back to Rule Catalog

Z401-DEEP-NESTING

Section titled “Z401-DEEP-NESTING”

Code nested 5+ levels deep.

  • Better: Use early returns and extract helper functions.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Hard to read and understand. Back to Rule Catalog

Z402-LONG-FUNCTIONS

Section titled “Z402-LONG-FUNCTIONS”

Functions > 50 lines.

  • Better: Extract smaller functions.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Hard to understand, test, and reuse. Back to Rule Catalog

Z403-INCONSISTENT-NAMING

Section titled “Z403-INCONSISTENT-NAMING”

Similar things named differently.

  • Better: Y300-EXPRESSIVE-NAMES consistently.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Confusing, hard to remember.
  • Bad Example: get_user, fetch_transaction, retrieve_account.

Back to Rule Catalog

Z404-COMMENTED-OUT-CODE

Section titled “Z404-COMMENTED-OUT-CODE”

Old code left commented out.

  • Better: Y310-REMOVE-DEAD-CODE, use version control.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Clutter and confusion about whether to use it. Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

index catalog

Catalog Overview

Section titled “Catalog Overview”
  • Guidelines
    • JY1xx — Language & Type Safety
      • JY10x — Language & Type Safety — Nullness, Optional, equality, records.
    • JY2xx — Code Organization & Design
      • JY20x — Code Organization & Design — Packaging, visibility, builders.
    • JY3xx — Error Handling & Logging
      • JY30x — Error Handling & Logging — Exception strategy and logging.
    • JY4xx — Concurrency & Immutability
      • JY40x — Concurrency & Immutability — Thread safety and synchronization.
    • JY5xx — Performance & Collections
      • JY50x — Performance & Collections — Collections and performance heuristics.
    • JY6xx — Testing & Tooling
      • JY61x — Isolation and Determinism — Fixtures, isolation, mocking, time control.
      • JY60x — Structure — Naming, AAA, parameterized tests, assertions.
    • JY7xx — Framework Addenda
      • JY73x — Android Testing — Robolectric and instrumentation guidance.
      • JY70x — Spring Testing — Spring test slices and context usage.
  • Anti-Patterns
    • JZ1xx — Null & Optional Misuse
      • JZ10x — Null & Optional Misuse — Null/Optional pitfalls.
    • JZ2xx — Exception Misuse
      • JZ20x — Exception Misuse — Bad exception handling.
    • JZ3xx — Concurrency Hazards
      • JZ30x — Concurrency Hazards — Threading hazards.
    • JZ4xx — Performance Anti-patterns
      • JZ40x — Performance Anti-patterns — Inefficient patterns.
    • JZ5xx — Testing Anti-patterns
      • JZ50x — Testing Anti-patterns — Testing pitfalls.

Guidelines

Section titled “Guidelines”

Normative rules to follow.

JY1xx — Language & Type Safety

Section titled “JY1xx — Language & Type Safety”

Nullness, equality, and records.

JY10x — Language & Type Safety

Section titled “JY10x — Language & Type Safety”

Nullness, Optional, equality, records.

RuleSummarySource
JY100-NULLNESS-CONTRACTSDefine and honor nullness contracts for public APIs. Nullness, Optional, equality, records.jy1xx/jy1xx-language-and-types.xml
JY101-OPTIONAL-RETURNSUse Optional&lt;T&gt; for return values that may be absent. Nullness, Optional, equality, records.jy1xx/jy1xx-language-and-types.xml
JY102-PREFER-PRIMITIVESPrefer primitives over boxed types unless nullability or collections require boxing. Nullness, Optional, equality, records.jy1xx/jy1xx-language-and-types.xml
JY103-EQUALS-HASHCODE-PAIROverride equals and hashCode together. Nullness, Optional, equality, records.jy1xx/jy1xx-language-and-types.xml
JY104-COMPARE-TO-CONSISTENTEnsure compareTo is consistent with equals. Nullness, Optional, equality, records.jy1xx/jy1xx-language-and-types.xml
JY105-RECORDS-FOR-DATAUse records for simple, immutable data carriers (Java 16+). Nullness, Optional, equality, records.jy1xx/jy1xx-language-and-types.xml

JY2xx — Code Organization & Design

Section titled “JY2xx — Code Organization & Design”

Packaging, visibility, and builders.

JY20x — Code Organization & Design

Section titled “JY20x — Code Organization & Design”

Packaging, visibility, builders.

RuleSummarySource
JY200-PACKAGE-BY-FEATUREOrganize packages by feature, not by technical layer. Packaging, visibility, builders.jy2xx/jy2xx-organization-and-design.xml
JY201-BOUNDARY-INTERFACESDefine interfaces at system boundaries; keep implementations package-private. Packaging, visibility, builders.jy2xx/jy2xx-organization-and-design.xml
JY202-MINIMUM-VISIBILITYUse the smallest visibility that works; avoid public fields. Packaging, visibility, builders.jy2xx/jy2xx-organization-and-design.xml
JY203-BUILDER-FOR-MANY-PARAMSUse builders when a constructor has more than 4 parameters. Packaging, visibility, builders.jy2xx/jy2xx-organization-and-design.xml
JY204-STATIC-FACTORY-NAMESPrefer named static factories over overloaded constructors. Packaging, visibility, builders.jy2xx/jy2xx-organization-and-design.xml
JY205-UTILITY-CLASS-DISCIPLINEUtility classes must be final with a private constructor. Packaging, visibility, builders.jy2xx/jy2xx-organization-and-design.xml

JY3xx — Error Handling & Logging

Section titled “JY3xx — Error Handling & Logging”

Exception strategy and logging.

JY30x — Error Handling & Logging

Section titled “JY30x — Error Handling & Logging”

Exception strategy and logging.

RuleSummarySource
JY300-EXCEPTION-STRATEGYUse unchecked exceptions for programmer errors; use checked exceptions only when callers can realistically recover. Exception strategy and logging.jy3xx/jy3xx-error-handling.xml
JY301-TRY-WITH-RESOURCESUse try-with-resources for AutoCloseable types. Exception strategy and logging.jy3xx/jy3xx-error-handling.xml
JY302-PRESERVE-CAUSEWrap exceptions with a meaningful message and original cause. Exception strategy and logging.jy3xx/jy3xx-error-handling.xml
JY303-DO-NOT-CATCH-TOO-BROADCatch the most specific exception type possible. Exception strategy and logging.jy3xx/jy3xx-error-handling.xml
JY304-LOG-ONCE-AT-BOUNDARYLog errors at boundaries (API, job runner) and avoid re-logging the same exception. Exception strategy and logging.jy3xx/jy3xx-error-handling.xml
JY305-VALIDATE-BOUNDARIESValidate external inputs at the boundary and fail fast. Exception strategy and logging.jy3xx/jy3xx-error-handling.xml

JY4xx — Concurrency & Immutability

Section titled “JY4xx — Concurrency & Immutability”

Thread safety and synchronization.

JY40x — Concurrency & Immutability

Section titled “JY40x — Concurrency & Immutability”

Thread safety and synchronization.

RuleSummarySource
JY400-PREFER-IMMUTABLE-DATAPrefer immutable objects and defensive copies across threads. Thread safety and synchronization.jy4xx/jy4xx-concurrency.xml
JY401-USE-CONCURRENT-UTILSUse java.util.concurrent utilities instead of manual synchronization. Thread safety and synchronization.jy4xx/jy4xx-concurrency.xml
JY402-LOCK-SCOPE-SMALLKeep synchronized sections minimal and lock on private objects. Thread safety and synchronization.jy4xx/jy4xx-concurrency.xml
JY403-ATOMIC-OR-SYNCUse Atomic* for simple counters; use synchronized or locks for compound invariants. Thread safety and synchronization.jy4xx/jy4xx-concurrency.xml
JY404-DOCUMENT-THREAD-SAFETYDocument whether a class is thread-safe or not. Thread safety and synchronization.jy4xx/jy4xx-concurrency.xml

JY5xx — Performance & Collections

Section titled “JY5xx — Performance & Collections”

Collections and performance heuristics.

JY50x — Performance & Collections

Section titled “JY50x — Performance & Collections”

Collections and performance heuristics.

RuleSummarySource
JY500-CHOOSE-COLLECTIONSPick collections based on access patterns and size. Collections and performance heuristics.jy5xx/jy5xx-performance.xml
JY501-STRINGBUILDER-IN-LOOPSUse StringBuilder for repeated concatenation in loops. Collections and performance heuristics.jy5xx/jy5xx-performance.xml
JY502-STREAMS-FOR-CLARITYUse streams for clear, side-effect-free pipelines. Prefer loops in hot paths. Collections and performance heuristics.jy5xx/jy5xx-performance.xml
JY503-AVOID-BOXING-IN-HOT-PATHSUse primitive types and primitive streams in tight loops. Collections and performance heuristics.jy5xx/jy5xx-performance.xml
JY504-MEASURE-BEFORE-OPTIMIZINGProfile before making performance changes. Collections and performance heuristics.jy5xx/jy5xx-performance.xml

JY6xx — Testing & Tooling

Section titled “JY6xx — Testing & Tooling”

JUnit testing practices.

JY61x — Isolation and Determinism

Section titled “JY61x — Isolation and Determinism”

Fixtures, isolation, mocking, time control.

RuleSummarySource
JY610-TEST-FIXTURESPrefer @BeforeEach for fixtures; avoid mutable shared state. Fixtures, isolation, mocking, time control.jy6xx/isolation.xml
JY611-TEST-ISOLATIONTests must not depend on execution order or shared state. Fixtures, isolation, mocking, time control.jy6xx/isolation.xml
JY612-MOCK-BOUNDARIESMock external systems (DB/HTTP/clock). Do not mock value objects or simple DTOs. Fixtures, isolation, mocking, time control.jy6xx/isolation.xml
JY613-DETERMINISMAvoid wall-clock time, random, and sleep in tests. Fixtures, isolation, mocking, time control.jy6xx/isolation.xml
JY614-TEST-LAYERINGSeparate unit and integration tests with tags or naming. Fixtures, isolation, mocking, time control.jy6xx/isolation.xml

JY60x — Structure

Section titled “JY60x — Structure”

Naming, AAA, parameterized tests, assertions.

RuleSummarySource
JY600-JUNIT5-DEFAULTUse JUnit 5 (org.junit.jupiter) as the default test runtime. Naming, AAA, parameterized tests, assertions.jy6xx/structure.xml
JY601-TEST-NAMINGUse descriptive test names that state behavior and context. Naming, AAA, parameterized tests, assertions.jy6xx/structure.xml
JY602-AAA-STRUCTUREStructure tests with Arrange/Act/Assert or Given/When/Then. Naming, AAA, parameterized tests, assertions.jy6xx/structure.xml
JY603-PARAMETERIZED-TESTSUse @ParameterizedTest for behavior with multiple inputs. Naming, AAA, parameterized tests, assertions.jy6xx/structure.xml
JY604-ASSERTION-STYLEPrefer fluent assertions (AssertJ). If not available, use JUnit assertions consistently. Naming, AAA, parameterized tests, assertions.jy6xx/structure.xml
JY605-EXCEPTION-ASSERTSUse assertThrows and assert on message or fields. Naming, AAA, parameterized tests, assertions.jy6xx/structure.xml

JY7xx — Framework Addenda

Section titled “JY7xx — Framework Addenda”

Spring and Android testing guidance.

JY73x — Android Testing

Section titled “JY73x — Android Testing”

Robolectric and instrumentation guidance.

RuleSummarySource
JY730-ROBOLECTRIC-DEFAULTPrefer Robolectric for JVM tests; use instrumented tests only when device APIs are required. Robolectric and instrumentation guidance.addenda/android-testing.xml
JY731-ANDROIDX-TEST-RUNNERUse AndroidX Test Runner for instrumented tests. Robolectric and instrumentation guidance.addenda/android-testing.xml
JY732-ESPRESSO-FOR-UIUse Espresso for UI behavior, but keep UI tests minimal. Robolectric and instrumentation guidance.addenda/android-testing.xml
JY733-IDLING-RESOURCESUse Idling Resources for async synchronization. Robolectric and instrumentation guidance.addenda/android-testing.xml

JY70x — Spring Testing

Section titled “JY70x — Spring Testing”

Spring test slices and context usage.

RuleSummarySource
JY700-TEST-SLICES-FIRSTPrefer test slices (@WebMvcTest, @DataJpaTest) over full context tests. Spring test slices and context usage.addenda/spring-testing.xml
JY701-SPRINGBOOTTEST-LIMITUse @SpringBootTest only for true integration tests. Spring test slices and context usage.addenda/spring-testing.xml
JY702-MOCK-BEANS-AT-BOUNDARYUse @MockBean for external collaborators, not for simple value objects. Spring test slices and context usage.addenda/spring-testing.xml
JY703-TESTCONFIGURATIONUse @TestConfiguration or @Import to provide test-only beans. Spring test slices and context usage.addenda/spring-testing.xml
JY704-CONTEXT-CACHINGAvoid dynamic properties that bust Spring context caching. Spring test slices and context usage.addenda/spring-testing.xml

Anti-Patterns

Section titled “Anti-Patterns”

Patterns to avoid.

JZ1xx — Null & Optional Misuse

Section titled “JZ1xx — Null & Optional Misuse”

Null/Optional pitfalls.

JZ10x — Null & Optional Misuse

Section titled “JZ10x — Null & Optional Misuse”

Null/Optional pitfalls.

RuleSummarySource
JZ100-NULL-RETURNSReturning null for missing values. Null/Optional pitfalls.jz1xx/jz1xx-null-optional.xml
JZ101-OPTIONAL-PARAMETERSUsing Optional as a method parameter or field. Null/Optional pitfalls.jz1xx/jz1xx-null-optional.xml
JZ102-NULL-COLLECTIONSReturning null instead of empty collections. Null/Optional pitfalls.jz1xx/jz1xx-null-optional.xml
JZ103-BOOLEAN-NULLUsing boxed Boolean where boolean suffices. Null/Optional pitfalls.jz1xx/jz1xx-null-optional.xml
JZ104-UNSAFE-OPTIONAL-GETCalling Optional.get() without checking presence. Null/Optional pitfalls.jz1xx/jz1xx-null-optional.xml

JZ2xx — Exception Misuse

Section titled “JZ2xx — Exception Misuse”

Bad exception handling.

JZ20x — Exception Misuse

Section titled “JZ20x — Exception Misuse”

Bad exception handling.

RuleSummarySource
JZ200-CATCH-EXCEPTIONCatching Exception or Throwable broadly. Bad exception handling.jz2xx/jz2xx-exception-misuse.xml
JZ201-SWALLOW-EXCEPTIONSEmpty catch blocks or ignoring errors. Bad exception handling.jz2xx/jz2xx-exception-misuse.xml
JZ202-LOSE-CAUSEThrowing a new exception without the original cause. Bad exception handling.jz2xx/jz2xx-exception-misuse.xml
JZ203-EXCEPTION-CONTROL-FLOWUsing exceptions for normal control flow. Bad exception handling.jz2xx/jz2xx-exception-misuse.xml

JZ3xx — Concurrency Hazards

Section titled “JZ3xx — Concurrency Hazards”

Threading hazards.

JZ30x — Concurrency Hazards

Section titled “JZ30x — Concurrency Hazards”

Threading hazards.

RuleSummarySource
JZ300-SHARED-MUTABLE-STATESharing mutable state across threads without coordination. Threading hazards.jz3xx/jz3xx-concurrency-hazards.xml
JZ301-LOCK-ON-PUBLICSynchronizing on this, strings, or other public objects. Threading hazards.jz3xx/jz3xx-concurrency-hazards.xml
JZ302-DOUBLE-CHECK-LOCKINGDouble-checked locking without volatile. Threading hazards.jz3xx/jz3xx-concurrency-hazards.xml
JZ303-THREAD-LEAKSCreating threads directly and forgetting to shut them down. Threading hazards.jz3xx/jz3xx-concurrency-hazards.xml

JZ4xx — Performance Anti-patterns

Section titled “JZ4xx — Performance Anti-patterns”

Inefficient patterns.

JZ40x — Performance Anti-patterns

Section titled “JZ40x — Performance Anti-patterns”

Inefficient patterns.

RuleSummarySource
JZ400-PREMATURE-OPTIMIZATIONOptimizing without evidence of a bottleneck. Inefficient patterns.jz4xx/jz4xx-performance-antipatterns.xml
JZ401-STREAMS-SIDE-EFFECTSUsing streams with side effects or shared mutable state. Inefficient patterns.jz4xx/jz4xx-performance-antipatterns.xml
JZ402-INEFFICIENT-CONTAINSUsing List.contains in hot paths with large lists. Inefficient patterns.jz4xx/jz4xx-performance-antipatterns.xml
JZ403-RECOMPILING-REGEXCompiling regex patterns repeatedly in loops. Inefficient patterns.jz4xx/jz4xx-performance-antipatterns.xml

JZ5xx — Testing Anti-patterns

Section titled “JZ5xx — Testing Anti-patterns”

Testing pitfalls.

JZ50x — Testing Anti-patterns

Section titled “JZ50x — Testing Anti-patterns”

Testing pitfalls.

RuleSummarySource
JZ500-TEST-INTERDEPENDENCETests rely on execution order or shared mutable state. Testing pitfalls.jz5xx/jz5xx-testing-antipatterns.xml
JZ501-OVER-MOCKINGMocking internals, value objects, or too many collaborators. Testing pitfalls.jz5xx/jz5xx-testing-antipatterns.xml
JZ502-ASSERTION-ROULETTEMultiple unrelated assertions without clear intent. Testing pitfalls.jz5xx/jz5xx-testing-antipatterns.xml
JZ503-EXCEPTION-SWALLOWCatching exceptions in tests without failing. Testing pitfalls.jz5xx/jz5xx-testing-antipatterns.xml
JZ504-UNSTABLE-TIMETests depend on real timezones or System.currentTimeMillis(). Testing pitfalls.jz5xx/jz5xx-testing-antipatterns.xml
JZ505-SLEEP-TESTSUsing Thread.sleep for synchronization. Testing pitfalls.jz5xx/jz5xx-testing-antipatterns.xml
JZ506-FLAKY-RANDOMNon-seeded randomness or data from external services. Testing pitfalls.jz5xx/jz5xx-testing-antipatterns.xml

View raw XML source

Android Testing

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
JY730-ROBOLECTRIC-DEFAULTPrefer Robolectric for JVM tests; use instrumented tests only when device APIs are required.
JY731-ANDROIDX-TEST-RUNNERUse AndroidX Test Runner for instrumented tests.
JY732-ESPRESSO-FOR-UIUse Espresso for UI behavior, but keep UI tests minimal.
JY733-IDLING-RESOURCESUse Idling Resources for async synchronization.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

JY730-ROBOLECTRIC-DEFAULT

Section titled “JY730-ROBOLECTRIC-DEFAULT”

Prefer Robolectric for JVM tests; use instrumented tests only when device APIs are required.

  • Rationale: Faster feedback and easier CI. Back to Rule Catalog

JY731-ANDROIDX-TEST-RUNNER

Section titled “JY731-ANDROIDX-TEST-RUNNER”

Use AndroidX Test Runner for instrumented tests.

  • Rationale: Standardizes test execution and reporting. Back to Rule Catalog

JY732-ESPRESSO-FOR-UI

Section titled “JY732-ESPRESSO-FOR-UI”

Use Espresso for UI behavior, but keep UI tests minimal.

  • Rationale: UI tests are slow and brittle. Back to Rule Catalog

JY733-IDLING-RESOURCES

Section titled “JY733-IDLING-RESOURCES”

Use Idling Resources for async synchronization.

  • Rationale: Avoids flaky waits and Thread.sleep. Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

Spring Testing

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
JY700-TEST-SLICES-FIRSTPrefer test slices (@WebMvcTest, @DataJpaTest) over full context tests.
JY701-SPRINGBOOTTEST-LIMITUse @SpringBootTest only for true integration tests.
JY702-MOCK-BEANS-AT-BOUNDARYUse @MockBean for external collaborators, not for simple value objects.
JY703-TESTCONFIGURATIONUse @TestConfiguration or @Import to provide test-only beans.
JY704-CONTEXT-CACHINGAvoid dynamic properties that bust Spring context caching.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

JY700-TEST-SLICES-FIRST

Section titled “JY700-TEST-SLICES-FIRST”

Prefer test slices (@WebMvcTest, @DataJpaTest) over full context tests.

  • Rationale: Faster and more focused tests. Back to Rule Catalog

JY701-SPRINGBOOTTEST-LIMIT

Section titled “JY701-SPRINGBOOTTEST-LIMIT”

Use @SpringBootTest only for true integration tests.

  • Rationale: Full context startup is slow and hides unit boundaries. Back to Rule Catalog

JY702-MOCK-BEANS-AT-BOUNDARY

Section titled “JY702-MOCK-BEANS-AT-BOUNDARY”

Use @MockBean for external collaborators, not for simple value objects.

  • Rationale: Keeps tests resilient to refactors. Back to Rule Catalog

JY703-TESTCONFIGURATION

Section titled “JY703-TESTCONFIGURATION”

Use @TestConfiguration or @Import to provide test-only beans.

  • Rationale: Avoids production code changes for tests. Back to Rule Catalog

JY704-CONTEXT-CACHING

Section titled “JY704-CONTEXT-CACHING”

Avoid dynamic properties that bust Spring context caching.

  • Rationale: Prevents unnecessary reboots of the app context. Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source
