Section titled “Source”

Related context files:

  • jy1xx-language-and-types.xml

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
Language & Type SafetyNullness, Optional, equality, records../jy1xx-language-and-types.xml

View raw XML source

Language & Type Safety

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
JY100-NULLNESS-CONTRACTSDefine and honor nullness contracts for public APIs.
JY101-OPTIONAL-RETURNSUse Optional&lt;T&gt; for return values that may be absent.
JY102-PREFER-PRIMITIVESPrefer primitives over boxed types unless nullability or collections require boxing.
JY103-EQUALS-HASHCODE-PAIROverride equals and hashCode together.
JY104-COMPARE-TO-CONSISTENTEnsure compareTo is consistent with equals.
JY105-RECORDS-FOR-DATAUse records for simple, immutable data carriers (Java 16+).

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

JY100-NULLNESS-CONTRACTS

Section titled “JY100-NULLNESS-CONTRACTS”

Define and honor nullness contracts for public APIs.

  • Rationale: Clear nullness avoids NPEs and makes call sites safer.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JZ100-NULL-RETURNS, JZ102-NULL-COLLECTIONS

Back to Rule Catalog

JY101-OPTIONAL-RETURNS

Section titled “JY101-OPTIONAL-RETURNS”

Use Optional&lt;T&gt; for return values that may be absent.

  • Rationale: Optional makes absence explicit and avoids null checks.

Examples

Section titled “Examples”
Good Example 1
Section titled “Good Example 1”
Bad Example 1
Section titled “Bad Example 1”
  • Related: JZ101-OPTIONAL-PARAMETERS, JZ104-UNSAFE-OPTIONAL-GET

Back to Rule Catalog

JY102-PREFER-PRIMITIVES

Section titled “JY102-PREFER-PRIMITIVES”

Prefer primitives over boxed types unless nullability or collections require boxing.

  • Rationale: Primitives avoid allocation and unexpected null.

Examples

Section titled “Examples”
Good Example 1
Section titled “Good Example 1”
Bad Example 1
Section titled “Bad Example 1”
  • Related: JZ103-BOOLEAN-NULL

Back to Rule Catalog

JY103-EQUALS-HASHCODE-PAIR

Section titled “JY103-EQUALS-HASHCODE-PAIR”

Override equals and hashCode together.

  • Rationale: Inconsistent equality breaks collections and caching.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: Y300-EXPRESSIVE-NAMES, Z403-INCONSISTENT-NAMING

Back to Rule Catalog

JY104-COMPARE-TO-CONSISTENT

Section titled “JY104-COMPARE-TO-CONSISTENT”

Ensure compareTo is consistent with equals.

  • Rationale: Inconsistency causes undefined behavior in sorted collections.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JY103-EQUALS-HASHCODE-PAIR

Back to Rule Catalog

JY105-RECORDS-FOR-DATA

Section titled “JY105-RECORDS-FOR-DATA”

Use records for simple, immutable data carriers (Java 16+).

  • Exceptions: Use classes when you need inheritance or custom serialization behavior.
  • Rationale: Records provide concise, correct value semantics.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”

Back to Rule Catalog

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

ItemSummaryOpen
Code Organization & DesignPackaging, visibility, builders../jy2xx-organization-and-design.xml

View raw XML source

Code Organization & Design

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
JY200-PACKAGE-BY-FEATUREOrganize packages by feature, not by technical layer.
JY201-BOUNDARY-INTERFACESDefine interfaces at system boundaries; keep implementations package-private.
JY202-MINIMUM-VISIBILITYUse the smallest visibility that works; avoid public fields.
JY203-BUILDER-FOR-MANY-PARAMSUse builders when a constructor has more than 4 parameters.
JY204-STATIC-FACTORY-NAMESPrefer named static factories over overloaded constructors.
JY205-UTILITY-CLASS-DISCIPLINEUtility classes must be final with a private constructor.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

JY200-PACKAGE-BY-FEATURE

Section titled “JY200-PACKAGE-BY-FEATURE”

Organize packages by feature, not by technical layer.

  • Rationale: Feature-based packages improve cohesion and local reasoning.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: Y106-MODULE-STRUCTURE, Y107-COHESION

Back to Rule Catalog

JY201-BOUNDARY-INTERFACES

Section titled “JY201-BOUNDARY-INTERFACES”

Define interfaces at system boundaries; keep implementations package-private.

  • Rationale: Interfaces enable testing, substitution, and stable contracts.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: Y101-INTERFACE-SEGREGATION, Y103-DEPENDENCY-INJECTION

Back to Rule Catalog

JY202-MINIMUM-VISIBILITY

Section titled “JY202-MINIMUM-VISIBILITY”

Use the smallest visibility that works; avoid public fields.

  • Rationale: Smaller surface area reduces coupling and misuse.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: Y108-COUPLING, Z100-TIGHT-COUPLING

Back to Rule Catalog

JY203-BUILDER-FOR-MANY-PARAMS

Section titled “JY203-BUILDER-FOR-MANY-PARAMS”

Use builders when a constructor has more than 4 parameters.

  • Rationale: Builders improve readability and reduce parameter order bugs.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: Y202-BUILDER-PATTERN

Back to Rule Catalog

JY204-STATIC-FACTORY-NAMES

Section titled “JY204-STATIC-FACTORY-NAMES”

Prefer named static factories over overloaded constructors.

  • Rationale: Names communicate intent and allow caching.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: Y200-FACTORY-METHOD

Back to Rule Catalog

JY205-UTILITY-CLASS-DISCIPLINE

Section titled “JY205-UTILITY-CLASS-DISCIPLINE”

Utility classes must be final with a private constructor.

  • Rationale: Prevents instantiation and subclassing of static helpers.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: Y111-UTILS-ANTIPATTERN

Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

Source

Section titled “Source”

Related context files:

  • jy3xx-error-handling.xml

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
Error Handling & LoggingException strategy and logging../jy3xx-error-handling.xml

View raw XML source

Error Handling & Logging

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
JY300-EXCEPTION-STRATEGYUse unchecked exceptions for programmer errors; use checked exceptions only when callers can realistically recover.
JY301-TRY-WITH-RESOURCESUse try-with-resources for AutoCloseable types.
JY302-PRESERVE-CAUSEWrap exceptions with a meaningful message and original cause.
JY303-DO-NOT-CATCH-TOO-BROADCatch the most specific exception type possible.
JY304-LOG-ONCE-AT-BOUNDARYLog errors at boundaries (API, job runner) and avoid re-logging the same exception.
JY305-VALIDATE-BOUNDARIESValidate external inputs at the boundary and fail fast.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

JY300-EXCEPTION-STRATEGY

Section titled “JY300-EXCEPTION-STRATEGY”

Use unchecked exceptions for programmer errors; use checked exceptions only when callers can realistically recover.

  • Rationale: Overusing checked exceptions increases boilerplate and hides root causes.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JZ200-CATCH-EXCEPTION

Back to Rule Catalog

JY301-TRY-WITH-RESOURCES

Section titled “JY301-TRY-WITH-RESOURCES”

Use try-with-resources for AutoCloseable types.

  • Rationale: Guarantees deterministic cleanup and avoids leaks.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JZ203-EXCEPTION-CONTROL-FLOW

Back to Rule Catalog

JY302-PRESERVE-CAUSE

Section titled “JY302-PRESERVE-CAUSE”

Wrap exceptions with a meaningful message and original cause.

  • Rationale: Preserves stack traces and root-cause analysis.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JZ202-LOSE-CAUSE

Back to Rule Catalog

JY303-DO-NOT-CATCH-TOO-BROAD

Section titled “JY303-DO-NOT-CATCH-TOO-BROAD”

Catch the most specific exception type possible.

  • Rationale: Broad catches hide unrelated failures and reduce reliability.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JZ200-CATCH-EXCEPTION

Back to Rule Catalog

JY304-LOG-ONCE-AT-BOUNDARY

Section titled “JY304-LOG-ONCE-AT-BOUNDARY”

Log errors at boundaries (API, job runner) and avoid re-logging the same exception.

  • Rationale: Duplicate logs obscure signal and inflate noise.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JZ201-SWALLOW-EXCEPTIONS

Back to Rule Catalog

JY305-VALIDATE-BOUNDARIES

Section titled “JY305-VALIDATE-BOUNDARIES”

Validate external inputs at the boundary and fail fast.

  • Rationale: Prevents invalid data from propagating into core logic.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: Y102-SEPARATION-OF-CONCERNS

Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

index

Source

Section titled “Source”

Related context files:

  • jy4xx-concurrency.xml

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
Concurrency & ImmutabilityThread safety and synchronization../jy4xx-concurrency.xml

View raw XML source

Concurrency & Immutability

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
JY400-PREFER-IMMUTABLE-DATAPrefer immutable objects and defensive copies across threads.
JY401-USE-CONCURRENT-UTILSUse java.util.concurrent utilities instead of manual synchronization.
JY402-LOCK-SCOPE-SMALLKeep synchronized sections minimal and lock on private objects.
JY403-ATOMIC-OR-SYNCUse Atomic* for simple counters; use synchronized or locks for compound invariants.
JY404-DOCUMENT-THREAD-SAFETYDocument whether a class is thread-safe or not.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

JY400-PREFER-IMMUTABLE-DATA

Section titled “JY400-PREFER-IMMUTABLE-DATA”

Prefer immutable objects and defensive copies across threads.

  • Rationale: Immutability eliminates entire classes of race conditions.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JZ300-SHARED-MUTABLE-STATE, Y113-COMPOSITION-OVER-INHERITANCE

Back to Rule Catalog

JY401-USE-CONCURRENT-UTILS

Section titled “JY401-USE-CONCURRENT-UTILS”

Use java.util.concurrent utilities instead of manual synchronization.

  • Rationale: Executors, queues, and atomics are safer and more scalable.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JZ303-THREAD-LEAKS

Back to Rule Catalog

JY402-LOCK-SCOPE-SMALL

Section titled “JY402-LOCK-SCOPE-SMALL”

Keep synchronized sections minimal and lock on private objects.

  • Rationale: Reduces contention and avoids accidental lock sharing.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JZ301-LOCK-ON-PUBLIC

Back to Rule Catalog

JY403-ATOMIC-OR-SYNC

Section titled “JY403-ATOMIC-OR-SYNC”

Use Atomic* for simple counters; use synchronized or locks for compound invariants.

  • Rationale: Atomics are fast but only safe for simple operations.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JZ302-DOUBLE-CHECK-LOCKING

Back to Rule Catalog

JY404-DOCUMENT-THREAD-SAFETY

Section titled “JY404-DOCUMENT-THREAD-SAFETY”

Document whether a class is thread-safe or not.

  • Rationale: Clear contracts avoid unsafe sharing and subtle bugs.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: Y300-EXPRESSIVE-NAMES

Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

index

Source

Section titled “Source”

Related context files:

  • jy5xx-performance.xml

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
Performance & CollectionsCollections and performance heuristics../jy5xx-performance.xml

View raw XML source

Performance & Collections

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
JY500-CHOOSE-COLLECTIONSPick collections based on access patterns and size.
JY501-STRINGBUILDER-IN-LOOPSUse StringBuilder for repeated concatenation in loops.
JY502-STREAMS-FOR-CLARITYUse streams for clear, side-effect-free pipelines.
JY503-AVOID-BOXING-IN-HOT-PATHSUse primitive types and primitive streams in tight loops.
JY504-MEASURE-BEFORE-OPTIMIZINGProfile before making performance changes.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

JY500-CHOOSE-COLLECTIONS

Section titled “JY500-CHOOSE-COLLECTIONS”

Pick collections based on access patterns and size.

  • Rationale: The wrong collection can cause hot-path bottlenecks.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JZ402-INEFFICIENT-CONTAINS

Back to Rule Catalog

JY501-STRINGBUILDER-IN-LOOPS

Section titled “JY501-STRINGBUILDER-IN-LOOPS”

Use StringBuilder for repeated concatenation in loops.

  • Rationale: Avoids quadratic string allocations.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JZ400-PREMATURE-OPTIMIZATION

Back to Rule Catalog

JY502-STREAMS-FOR-CLARITY

Section titled “JY502-STREAMS-FOR-CLARITY”

Use streams for clear, side-effect-free pipelines. Prefer loops in hot paths.

  • Rationale: Streams improve readability but can add overhead.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JZ401-STREAMS-SIDE-EFFECTS

Back to Rule Catalog

JY503-AVOID-BOXING-IN-HOT-PATHS

Section titled “JY503-AVOID-BOXING-IN-HOT-PATHS”

Use primitive types and primitive streams in tight loops.

  • Rationale: Boxing creates allocation and GC pressure.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JY102-PREFER-PRIMITIVES

Back to Rule Catalog

JY504-MEASURE-BEFORE-OPTIMIZING

Section titled “JY504-MEASURE-BEFORE-OPTIMIZING”

Profile before making performance changes.

  • Rationale: Most performance issues are not where you expect.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: JZ400-PREMATURE-OPTIMIZATION

Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

index

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
StructureNaming, AAA, parameterized tests, assertions../structure.xml
Isolation and DeterminismFixtures, isolation, mocking, time control../isolation.xml

View raw XML source

Isolation and Determinism

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
JY610-TEST-FIXTURESPrefer @BeforeEach for fixtures; avoid mutable shared state.
JY611-TEST-ISOLATIONTests must not depend on execution order or shared state.
JY612-MOCK-BOUNDARIESMock external systems (DB/HTTP/clock).
JY613-DETERMINISMAvoid wall-clock time, random, and sleep in tests.
JY614-TEST-LAYERINGSeparate unit and integration tests with tags or naming.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

JY610-TEST-FIXTURES

Section titled “JY610-TEST-FIXTURES”

Prefer @BeforeEach for fixtures; avoid mutable shared state.

  • Rationale: Isolated tests are deterministic and parallel-safe.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”

Back to Rule Catalog

JY611-TEST-ISOLATION

Section titled “JY611-TEST-ISOLATION”

Tests must not depend on execution order or shared state.

  • Rationale: Order dependence causes flaky CI behavior.
  • Good Example: Use fresh data builders per test.

Back to Rule Catalog

JY612-MOCK-BOUNDARIES

Section titled “JY612-MOCK-BOUNDARIES”

Mock external systems (DB/HTTP/clock). Do not mock value objects or simple DTOs.

  • Rationale: Over-mocking couples tests to implementation details.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”

Back to Rule Catalog

JY613-DETERMINISM

Section titled “JY613-DETERMINISM”

Avoid wall-clock time, random, and sleep in tests.

  • Rationale: Nondeterminism causes flaky tests.
  • Good Example: Inject Clock, seed Random, or use fakes.

Back to Rule Catalog

JY614-TEST-LAYERING

Section titled “JY614-TEST-LAYERING”

Separate unit and integration tests with tags or naming.

  • Rationale: Keeps fast feedback loops while retaining coverage.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”

Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

Structure

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
JY600-JUNIT5-DEFAULTUse JUnit 5 (org.junit.jupiter) as the default test runtime.
JY601-TEST-NAMINGUse descriptive test names that state behavior and context.
JY602-AAA-STRUCTUREStructure tests with Arrange/Act/Assert or Given/When/Then.
JY603-PARAMETERIZED-TESTSUse @ParameterizedTest for behavior with multiple inputs.
JY604-ASSERTION-STYLEPrefer fluent assertions (AssertJ).
JY605-EXCEPTION-ASSERTSUse assertThrows and assert on message or fields.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

JY600-JUNIT5-DEFAULT

Section titled “JY600-JUNIT5-DEFAULT”

Use JUnit 5 (org.junit.jupiter) as the default test runtime.

  • Exceptions: If a project is still on JUnit 4, isolate legacy tests and migrate incrementally.
  • Rationale: JUnit 5 is the current standard with better extensions and parameterized tests.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”

Back to Rule Catalog

JY601-TEST-NAMING

Section titled “JY601-TEST-NAMING”

Use descriptive test names that state behavior and context.

  • Rationale: Names are the primary documentation for test intent.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”
  • Related: Y300-EXPRESSIVE-NAMES

Back to Rule Catalog

JY602-AAA-STRUCTURE

Section titled “JY602-AAA-STRUCTURE”

Structure tests with Arrange/Act/Assert or Given/When/Then.

  • Rationale: Consistent structure improves readability and review speed.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”

Back to Rule Catalog

JY603-PARAMETERIZED-TESTS

Section titled “JY603-PARAMETERIZED-TESTS”

Use @ParameterizedTest for behavior with multiple inputs.

  • Rationale: Reduces duplication and improves coverage.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”

Back to Rule Catalog

JY604-ASSERTION-STYLE

Section titled “JY604-ASSERTION-STYLE”

Prefer fluent assertions (AssertJ). If not available, use JUnit assertions consistently.

  • Rationale: Fluent assertions produce clearer failures and intent.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”

Back to Rule Catalog

JY605-EXCEPTION-ASSERTS

Section titled “JY605-EXCEPTION-ASSERTS”

Use assertThrows and assert on message or fields.

  • Rationale: Verifies both error type and meaning.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”

Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

Sources

Section titled “Sources”

No entries.

Spring TestingSpring test slices and context usage.../addenda/spring-testing.xml
Android TestingRobolectric and instrumentation guidance.../addenda/android-testing.xml

Source

Section titled “Source”

Related context files:

  • jz1xx-null-optional.xml

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
Null & Optional MisuseNull/Optional pitfalls../jz1xx-null-optional.xml

View raw XML source

Null & Optional Misuse

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
JZ100-NULL-RETURNSReturning null for missing values.
JZ101-OPTIONAL-PARAMETERSUsing Optional as a method parameter or field.
JZ102-NULL-COLLECTIONSReturning null instead of empty collections.
JZ103-BOOLEAN-NULLUsing boxed Boolean where boolean suffices.
JZ104-UNSAFE-OPTIONAL-GETCalling Optional.get() without checking presence.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

JZ100-NULL-RETURNS

Section titled “JZ100-NULL-RETURNS”

Returning null for missing values.

  • Better: JY101-OPTIONAL-RETURNS JZ102-NULL-COLLECTIONS
  • Why This Is Problematic: Forces every caller to remember null checks. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ101-OPTIONAL-PARAMETERS

Section titled “JZ101-OPTIONAL-PARAMETERS”

Using Optional as a method parameter or field.

  • Better: Overload methods or use nullable annotations on params.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Adds noise and encourages null anyway. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ102-NULL-COLLECTIONS

Section titled “JZ102-NULL-COLLECTIONS”

Returning null instead of empty collections.

  • Better: Return List.of(), Set.of(), or Collections.emptyList().
  • Why This Is Problematic: Surprising and error-prone for callers. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ103-BOOLEAN-NULL

Section titled “JZ103-BOOLEAN-NULL”

Using boxed Boolean where boolean suffices.

  • Better: Use primitive boolean or Optional&lt;Boolean&gt; for tri-state.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Introduces null tri-state and NPEs. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ104-UNSAFE-OPTIONAL-GET

Section titled “JZ104-UNSAFE-OPTIONAL-GET”

Calling Optional.get() without checking presence.

  • Better: Use orElse, orElseGet, or orElseThrow with a message.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Throws NoSuchElementException at runtime. Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

index

Source

Section titled “Source”

Related context files:

  • jz2xx-exception-misuse.xml

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
Exception MisuseBad exception handling../jz2xx-exception-misuse.xml

View raw XML source

Exception Misuse

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
JZ200-CATCH-EXCEPTIONCatching Exception or Throwable broadly.
JZ201-SWALLOW-EXCEPTIONSEmpty catch blocks or ignoring errors.
JZ202-LOSE-CAUSEThrowing a new exception without the original cause.
JZ203-EXCEPTION-CONTROL-FLOWUsing exceptions for normal control flow.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

JZ200-CATCH-EXCEPTION

Section titled “JZ200-CATCH-EXCEPTION”

Catching Exception or Throwable broadly.

  • Better: Catch specific exception types (JY303-DO-NOT-CATCH-TOO-BROAD).
  • Why This Is Problematic: Masks unexpected failures and makes recovery unsafe. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ201-SWALLOW-EXCEPTIONS

Section titled “JZ201-SWALLOW-EXCEPTIONS”

Empty catch blocks or ignoring errors.

  • Better: Log at boundary and rethrow or handle explicitly.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Hides failures and complicates debugging. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ202-LOSE-CAUSE

Section titled “JZ202-LOSE-CAUSE”

Throwing a new exception without the original cause.

  • Better: JY302-PRESERVE-CAUSE
  • Why This Is Problematic: Loses stack trace and root-cause context. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ203-EXCEPTION-CONTROL-FLOW

Section titled “JZ203-EXCEPTION-CONTROL-FLOW”

Using exceptions for normal control flow.

  • Better: Use conditionals or return types to model absence.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Slower, noisy, and obscures intent. Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

index

Source

Section titled “Source”

Related context files:

  • jz3xx-concurrency-hazards.xml

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
Concurrency HazardsThreading hazards../jz3xx-concurrency-hazards.xml

View raw XML source

Concurrency Hazards

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
JZ300-SHARED-MUTABLE-STATESharing mutable state across threads without coordination.
JZ301-LOCK-ON-PUBLICSynchronizing on this, strings, or other public objects.
JZ302-DOUBLE-CHECK-LOCKINGDouble-checked locking without volatile.
JZ303-THREAD-LEAKSCreating threads directly and forgetting to shut them down.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

JZ300-SHARED-MUTABLE-STATE

Section titled “JZ300-SHARED-MUTABLE-STATE”

Sharing mutable state across threads without coordination.

  • Better: JY400-PREFER-IMMUTABLE-DATA JY401-USE-CONCURRENT-UTILS
  • Why This Is Problematic: Data races and inconsistent reads. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ301-LOCK-ON-PUBLIC

Section titled “JZ301-LOCK-ON-PUBLIC”

Synchronizing on this, strings, or other public objects.

  • Better: Use a private lock object (JY402-LOCK-SCOPE-SMALL).
  • Why This Is Problematic: External code can acquire the same lock and cause deadlocks. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ302-DOUBLE-CHECK-LOCKING

Section titled “JZ302-DOUBLE-CHECK-LOCKING”

Double-checked locking without volatile.

  • Better: Use volatile, static final, or Initialization-on-demand.
  • Why This Is Problematic: May publish partially constructed objects. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ303-THREAD-LEAKS

Section titled “JZ303-THREAD-LEAKS”

Creating threads directly and forgetting to shut them down.

  • Better: Use ExecutorService and call shutdown().
  • Why This Is Problematic: Leaks resources and prevents clean shutdown. Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

index

Source

Section titled “Source”

Related context files:

  • jz4xx-performance-antipatterns.xml

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
Performance Anti-patternsInefficient patterns../jz4xx-performance-antipatterns.xml

View raw XML source

Performance Anti-patterns

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
JZ400-PREMATURE-OPTIMIZATIONOptimizing without evidence of a bottleneck.
JZ401-STREAMS-SIDE-EFFECTSUsing streams with side effects or shared mutable state.
JZ402-INEFFICIENT-CONTAINSUsing List.contains in hot paths with large lists.
JZ403-RECOMPILING-REGEXCompiling regex patterns repeatedly in loops.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

JZ400-PREMATURE-OPTIMIZATION

Section titled “JZ400-PREMATURE-OPTIMIZATION”

Optimizing without evidence of a bottleneck.

  • Better: JY504-MEASURE-BEFORE-OPTIMIZING
  • Why This Is Problematic: Adds complexity and hides real issues. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ401-STREAMS-SIDE-EFFECTS

Section titled “JZ401-STREAMS-SIDE-EFFECTS”

Using streams with side effects or shared mutable state.

  • Better: Keep stream operations pure or use a loop.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Hard to reason about and unsafe for parallel streams. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ402-INEFFICIENT-CONTAINS

Section titled “JZ402-INEFFICIENT-CONTAINS”

Using List.contains in hot paths with large lists.

  • Better: Use HashSet or HashMap for membership tests.
  • Why This Is Problematic: O(n) lookups in tight loops. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ403-RECOMPILING-REGEX

Section titled “JZ403-RECOMPILING-REGEX”

Compiling regex patterns repeatedly in loops.

  • Better: Cache a Pattern as static final.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Pattern compilation is expensive. Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

index

Source

Section titled “Source”

Related context files:

  • jz5xx-testing-antipatterns.xml

Groups

Section titled “Groups”

Group-level groupings within a Categories bucket.

ItemSummaryOpen
Testing Anti-patternsTesting pitfalls../jz5xx-testing-antipatterns.xml

View raw XML source

Testing Anti-patterns

Rule Catalog

Section titled “Rule Catalog”
RuleSummary
JZ500-TEST-INTERDEPENDENCETests rely on execution order or shared mutable state.
JZ501-OVER-MOCKINGMocking internals, value objects, or too many collaborators.
JZ502-ASSERTION-ROULETTEMultiple unrelated assertions without clear intent.
JZ503-EXCEPTION-SWALLOWCatching exceptions in tests without failing.
JZ504-UNSTABLE-TIMETests depend on real timezones or System.currentTimeMillis().
JZ505-SLEEP-TESTSUsing Thread.sleep for synchronization.
JZ506-FLAKY-RANDOMNon-seeded randomness or data from external services.

Rule Details

Section titled “Rule Details”

JZ500-TEST-INTERDEPENDENCE

Section titled “JZ500-TEST-INTERDEPENDENCE”

Tests rely on execution order or shared mutable state.

  • Better: JY610-TEST-FIXTURES JY611-TEST-ISOLATION
  • Why This Is Problematic: Produces flaky failures in CI. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ501-OVER-MOCKING

Section titled “JZ501-OVER-MOCKING”

Mocking internals, value objects, or too many collaborators.

  • Better: JY612-MOCK-BOUNDARIES
  • Why This Is Problematic: Tests become brittle and mirror implementation. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ502-ASSERTION-ROULETTE

Section titled “JZ502-ASSERTION-ROULETTE”

Multiple unrelated assertions without clear intent.

  • Better: JY601-TEST-NAMING JY602-AAA-STRUCTURE
  • Why This Is Problematic: Failures are hard to diagnose. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ503-EXCEPTION-SWALLOW

Section titled “JZ503-EXCEPTION-SWALLOW”

Catching exceptions in tests without failing.

  • Better: Use assertThrows (JY605-EXCEPTION-ASSERTS)
  • Why This Is Problematic: Hides regressions. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ504-UNSTABLE-TIME

Section titled “JZ504-UNSTABLE-TIME”

Tests depend on real timezones or System.currentTimeMillis().

  • Better: JY613-DETERMINISM
  • Why This Is Problematic: Time-based flakiness. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ505-SLEEP-TESTS

Section titled “JZ505-SLEEP-TESTS”

Using Thread.sleep for synchronization.

  • Better: Use latches, awaitility, or fakes.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Slow and flaky. Back to Rule Catalog

JZ506-FLAKY-RANDOM

Section titled “JZ506-FLAKY-RANDOM”

Non-seeded randomness or data from external services.

  • Better: Seed randomness or use fixed fixtures.
  • Why This Is Problematic: Non-reproducible failures. Back to Rule Catalog

View raw XML source

index catalog

Catalog Overview

Section titled “Catalog Overview”
  • Guidelines
    • PY1xx — Type Hints & Type Safety
      • PY10x — Basics — Core annotation usage and type specificity.
      • PY11x — Type System Patterns — Unions, narrowing, generics, strictness.
      • PY12x — Typing Constructs — Contracts, overloads, advanced typing.
    • PY2xx — Code Organization & Modularity
      • PY20x — Public API and Interfaces — Exports, privacy, package contracts.
      • PY21x — Imports and Packaging — Import style, ordering, packaging rules.
    • PY3xx — Functional Programming
      • PY30x — Immutability and Purity — Immutable data, pure functions, generators.
      • PY31x — Functional Tooling — Itertools, operator, dataclass replace, HOFs.
      • PY32x — Advanced Techniques — Referential transparency, pipelines, monads.
    • PY4xx — Documentation & Readability
      • PY40x — Docstrings and Documentation — Docstring format, coverage, layout.
      • PY41x — Formatting and Strings — Quotes, f-strings, concatenation.
    • PY5xx — Error Handling
      • PY50x — Exception Types and Boundaries — Specific exceptions, hierarchy, context.
      • PY51x — Control Flow and Messages — Logging, assertions, validation, messages.
    • PY6xx — Performance & Best Practices
      • PY60x — Profiling and Structure — Measure first, generators, caching, layout.
      • PY61x — Micro-optimizations — Built-ins, regex, sets, lazy imports.
  • Anti-Patterns
    • PZ1xx — Type Safety Violations
      • PZ10x — Missing or Weak Types — Any overuse, missing hints, weak contracts.
      • PZ11x — Type Violations — Variance, runtime misuse, dataclass misuse.
    • PZ2xx — Mutability Problems
      • PZ20x — Shared State Issues — Globals, shared mutable state, caching.
      • PZ21x — Mutation Hazards — In-place ops, loop mutation, frozen violations.
    • PZ3xx — Miscellaneous
      • PZ30x — Miscellaneous — Other Python-specific issues.

Guidelines

Section titled “Guidelines”

Normative rules to follow.

PY1xx — Type Hints & Type Safety

Section titled “PY1xx — Type Hints & Type Safety”

Typing rules and contracts.

PY10x — Basics

Section titled “PY10x — Basics”

Core annotation usage and type specificity.

RuleSummarySource
PY100-USE-TYPE-HINTSAlways use type hints for function parameters, return values, and class attributes. Core annotation usage and type specificity.py1xx/py10x-basics/py100-use-type-hints.xml
PY101-PRECISE-TYPESUse the most specific type possible. Prefer Sequence[str] over list, Mapping[str, int] over dict. Core annotation usage and type specificity.py1xx/py10x-basics/py101-precise-types.xml
PY102-USE-PROTOCOLSPrefer Protocol over ABC for structural subtyping. Use ABC only when inheritance is semantically important. Core annotation usage and type specificity.py1xx/py10x-basics/py102-use-protocols.xml
PY103-USE-GENERICSUse generic types for reusable components that work with multiple types. Prefer Python 3.12+ native syntax with automatic variance inference. Core annotation usage and type specificity.py1xx/py10x-basics/py103-use-generics.xml
PY104-TYPE-ALIASESUse type aliases for complex types. Use TypeAlias annotation for clarity. Core annotation usage and type specificity.py1xx/py10x-basics/py104-type-aliases.xml
PY105-NEWTYPEUse NewType to create distinct types from primitives when semantic distinction is important. Core annotation usage and type specificity.py1xx/py10x-basics/py105-newtype.xml
PY106-COLLECTION-TYPESUse appropriate collection types from collections.abc: Sequence, Mapping, Iterable, Iterator, Collection. Core annotation usage and type specificity.py1xx/py10x-basics/py106-collection-types.xml
PY107-LITERAL-TYPESUse Literal for values that must be specific constants. Core annotation usage and type specificity.py1xx/py10x-basics/py107-literal-types.xml
PY108-FINALUse Final for constants that should never be reassigned. Core annotation usage and type specificity.py1xx/py10x-basics/py108-final.xml
PY109-AVOID-ANYAvoid Any type unless absolutely necessary. Use generics, unions, or protocols instead. Core annotation usage and type specificity.py1xx/py10x-basics/py109-avoid-any.xml

PY11x — Type System Patterns

Section titled “PY11x — Type System Patterns”

Unions, narrowing, generics, strictness.

RuleSummarySource
PY110-UNION-TYPESUse modern union syntax X &#124; Y (Python 3.10+) instead of Union[X, Y] or Optional[X]. Unions, narrowing, generics, strictness.py1xx/py11x-generics/py110-union-types.xml
PY111-TYPE-NARROWINGUse type narrowing with isinstance(), is None checks, and TypeGuard for complex narrowing. Unions, narrowing, generics, strictness.py1xx/py11x-generics/py111-type-narrowing.xml
PY112-DATACLASS-TYPESUse dataclasses with full type hints for data containers. Unions, narrowing, generics, strictness.py1xx/py11x-generics/py112-dataclass-types.xml
PY113-CALLABLE-TYPESUse Callable type hint for functions passed as parameters. Unions, narrowing, generics, strictness.py1xx/py11x-generics/py113-callable-types.xml
PY114-NAMEDTUPLE-TYPESUse NamedTuple with type hints for simple immutable data structures. Unions, narrowing, generics, strictness.py1xx/py11x-generics/py114-namedtuple-types.xml
PY115-RETURN-TYPE-HINTSAlways specify return types, even for functions returning None. Unions, narrowing, generics, strictness.py1xx/py11x-generics/py115-return-type-hints.xml
PY116-CLASS-VARUse ClassVar to annotate class variables (not instance variables). Unions, narrowing, generics, strictness.py1xx/py11x-generics/py116-class-var.xml
PY117-SELF-TYPEUse Self type for methods returning the instance (method chaining, factory methods). Unions, narrowing, generics, strictness.py1xx/py11x-generics/py117-self-type.xml
PY118-STRICT-MODEEnable mypy strict mode in your project configuration. Unions, narrowing, generics, strictness.py1xx/py11x-generics/py118-strict-mode.xml
PY119-TYPE-COMMENTSAvoid type comments. Use modern annotation syntax instead. Unions, narrowing, generics, strictness.py1xx/py11x-generics/py119-type-comments.xml

PY12x — Typing Constructs

Section titled “PY12x — Typing Constructs”

Contracts, overloads, advanced typing.

RuleSummarySource
PY120-TYPE-CHECKINGUse if TYPE_CHECKING: for import-only type hints to avoid circular imports. Contracts, overloads, advanced typing.py1xx/py12x-typing-constructs/py120-type-checking.xml
PY121-FORWARD-REFSUse forward references (string literals) for types not yet defined or circular dependencies. Contracts, overloads, advanced typing.py1xx/py12x-typing-constructs/py121-forward-refs.xml
PY122-OVERLOADUse @overload for functions with different signatures based on argument types. Contracts, overloads, advanced typing.py1xx/py12x-typing-constructs/py122-overload.xml
PY123-PARAMSPECUse ParamSpec to preserve function signatures in decorators. Contracts, overloads, advanced typing.py1xx/py12x-typing-constructs/py123-paramspec.xml
PY124-CONCATENATEUse Concatenate to add parameters to callables (useful for method decorators). Contracts, overloads, advanced typing.py1xx/py12x-typing-constructs/py124-concatenate.xml
PY125-ENFORCE-IMMUTABILITY-VIA-TYPESEnforce immutability through type hints (e.g., Sequence, Mapping, AbstractSet) rather than creating immutable copies of collections. Contracts, overloads, advanced typing.py1xx/py12x-typing-constructs/py125-enforce-immutability-via-types.xml
PY126-USE-METACLASS-ABCMETAWhen using abstract base classes, use metaclass=ABCMeta with @abstractmethod decorator and ellipsis body. Contracts, overloads, advanced typing.py1xx/py12x-typing-constructs/py126-use-metaclass-abcmeta.xml
PY127-DEFINE-CONTRACTS-IN-CODEDefine contracts explicitly in code (class attributes with __init__ methods, abstract properties) instead of documenting them in docstrings. Contracts, overloads, advanced typing.py1xx/py12x-typing-constructs/py127-define-contracts-in-code.xml
PY128-NO-EXPORT-TYPEVARSNever export TypeVar definitions in module all; TypeVars are implementation details, not public API. Contracts, overloads, advanced typing.py1xx/py12x-typing-constructs/py128-no-export-typevars.xml
PY129-PREFER-PUBLIC-ATTRIBUTESPrefer public attributes for simple stored values over private attributes with @property decorators. Contracts, overloads, advanced typing.py1xx/py12x-typing-constructs/py129-prefer-public-attributes.xml

PY2xx — Code Organization & Modularity

Section titled “PY2xx — Code Organization & Modularity”

Packaging, imports, and public API.

PY20x — Public API and Interfaces

Section titled “PY20x — Public API and Interfaces”

Exports, privacy, package contracts.

RuleSummarySource
PY200-PACKAGE-INITUse __init__.py to expose public API using from ... import *. Exports, privacy, package contracts.py2xx/py20x-public-api.xml
PY201-PUBLIC-APIDefine __all__ in each module (not in __init__.py) to control the public API. Exports, privacy, package contracts.py2xx/py20x-public-api.xml
PY202-EXPLICIT-EXPORTSBe explicit when importing from other modules. Avoid import * in application code. Exports, privacy, package contracts.py2xx/py20x-public-api.xml
PY203-PRIVATE-MODULESUse _module.py prefix for internal/private modules. Exports, privacy, package contracts.py2xx/py20x-public-api.xml
PY204-INIT-DEFINES-BEHAVIORConstructor parameters define instance behavior and configuration; method parameters are call-specific data that varies per invocation. Exports, privacy, package contracts.py2xx/py20x-public-api.xml

PY21x — Imports and Packaging

Section titled “PY21x — Imports and Packaging”

Import style, ordering, packaging rules.

RuleSummarySource
PY210-IMPORT-STYLEPrefer absolute imports for public modules; use relative imports for private (underscore-prefixed) modules within packages. Import style, ordering, packaging rules.py2xx/py21x-imports-and-packaging.xml
PY211-IMPORT-ORDERStandard library, third-party, local. Alphabetical within groups. Import style, ordering, packaging rules.py2xx/py21x-imports-and-packaging.xml
PY212-CIRCULAR-IMPORTSAvoid circular dependencies. Use protocols or TYPE_CHECKING. Import style, ordering, packaging rules.py2xx/py21x-imports-and-packaging.xml
PY213-LAZY-IMPORTSImport at module level by default. Use local imports only for optional dependencies or circular imports. Import style, ordering, packaging rules.py2xx/py21x-imports-and-packaging.xml
PY214-TYPE-STUBSFor untyped third-party libs, use stub files or py.typed. Import style, ordering, packaging rules.py2xx/py21x-imports-and-packaging.xml
PY215-NAMESPACE-PACKAGESAvoid namespace packages unless distributing plugins. Import style, ordering, packaging rules.py2xx/py21x-imports-and-packaging.xml

PY3xx — Functional Programming

Section titled “PY3xx — Functional Programming”

Immutability and functional techniques.

PY30x — Immutability and Purity

Section titled “PY30x — Immutability and Purity”

Immutable data, pure functions, generators.

RuleSummarySource
PY300-IMMUTABILITYPrefer immutable data structures. Use frozen=True for dataclasses. Immutable data, pure functions, generators.py3xx/py30x-immutability-and-purity/py300-immutability.xml
PY301-FROZEN-DATACLASSESUse frozen=True for dataclasses that shouldn’t change. Immutable data, pure functions, generators.py3xx/py30x-immutability-and-purity/py301-frozen-dataclasses.xml
PY302-PURE-FUNCTIONSFunctions without side effects, same input → same output. Immutable data, pure functions, generators.py3xx/py30x-immutability-and-purity/py302-pure-functions.xml
PY303-AVOID-SIDE-EFFECTSDon’t modify arguments or global state. Return new values. Immutable data, pure functions, generators.py3xx/py30x-immutability-and-purity/py303-avoid-side-effects.xml
PY304-RETURN-NEW-VALUESReturn new objects instead of modifying existing ones. Immutable data, pure functions, generators.py3xx/py30x-immutability-and-purity/py304-return-new-values.xml
PY305-GENERATORSUse generators with yield for lazy evaluation and memory efficiency. Generators compute values on-demand rather than creating entire sequences in memory. Immutable data, pure functions, generators.py3xx/py30x-immutability-and-purity/py305-generators.xml
PY306-LAZY-EVALUATIONPrefer lazy evaluation over eager computation. Compute values only when actually needed. This reduces memory usage, enables working with infinite sequences, and can improve performance by avoiding unnecessary computation. Immutable data, pure functions, generators.py3xx/py30x-immutability-and-purity/py306-lazy-evaluation.xml
PY307-COMPREHENSIONSUse list/dict/set comprehensions for transformations. Immutable data, pure functions, generators.py3xx/py30x-immutability-and-purity/py307-comprehensions.xml
PY308-MAP-FILTERPrefer comprehensions over map()/filter() for clarity. Immutable data, pure functions, generators.py3xx/py30x-immutability-and-purity/py308-map-filter.xml
PY309-FUNCTOOLSUse functools for functional patterns: reduce, partial, lru_cache. Immutable data, pure functions, generators.py3xx/py30x-immutability-and-purity/py309-functools.xml

PY31x — Functional Tooling

Section titled “PY31x — Functional Tooling”

Itertools, operator, dataclass replace, HOFs.

RuleSummarySource
PY310-ITERTOOLSUse itertools for efficient iteration patterns. Itertools, operator, dataclass replace, HOFs.py3xx/py31x-functional-tooling.xml
PY311-OPERATOR-MODULEUse operator module instead of lambdas for common operations. Itertools, operator, dataclass replace, HOFs.py3xx/py31x-functional-tooling.xml
PY312-DATACLASS-REPLACEUse dataclasses.replace() for immutable updates. Itertools, operator, dataclass replace, HOFs.py3xx/py31x-functional-tooling.xml
PY313-AVOID-MUTABLE-DEFAULTSNever use mutable default arguments. Itertools, operator, dataclass replace, HOFs.py3xx/py31x-functional-tooling.xml
PY314-CLOSURESUse closures for encapsulation and factory functions. Itertools, operator, dataclass replace, HOFs.py3xx/py31x-functional-tooling.xml
PY315-FIRST-CLASS-FUNCTIONSPass functions as arguments, return from functions. Itertools, operator, dataclass replace, HOFs.py3xx/py31x-functional-tooling.xml
PY316-HIGHER-ORDER-FUNCTIONSFunctions that take or return functions. Itertools, operator, dataclass replace, HOFs.py3xx/py31x-functional-tooling.xml
PY317-RECURSIONUse recursion for naturally recursive problems (trees, etc.). Itertools, operator, dataclass replace, HOFs.py3xx/py31x-functional-tooling.xml
PY318-TAIL-RECURSIONPython doesn’t optimize tail recursion. Use iteration instead. Itertools, operator, dataclass replace, HOFs.py3xx/py31x-functional-tooling.xml
PY319-IMMUTABLE-COLLECTIONSUse tuple, frozenset for immutable collections. Itertools, operator, dataclass replace, HOFs.py3xx/py31x-functional-tooling.xml

PY32x — Advanced Techniques

Section titled “PY32x — Advanced Techniques”

Referential transparency, pipelines, monads.

RuleSummarySource
PY320-AVOID-GLOBALSMinimize global mutable state. Use parameters/returns. Referential transparency, pipelines, monads.py3xx/py32x-advanced.xml
PY321-REFERENTIAL-TRANSPARENCYFunction calls can be replaced with their return values. Referential transparency, pipelines, monads.py3xx/py32x-advanced.xml
PY322-PIPELINE-PATTERNChain transformations using function composition. Referential transparency, pipelines, monads.py3xx/py32x-advanced.xml
PY323-MONADSConsider Result/Option types for error handling (via libraries). Referential transparency, pipelines, monads.py3xx/py32x-advanced.xml
PY324-PARTIAL-APPLICATIONUse functools.partial to pre-fill function arguments. Referential transparency, pipelines, monads.py3xx/py32x-advanced.xml

PY4xx — Documentation & Readability

Section titled “PY4xx — Documentation & Readability”

Docstrings and formatting.

PY40x — Docstrings and Documentation

Section titled “PY40x — Docstrings and Documentation”

Docstring format, coverage, layout.

RuleSummarySource
PY400-RST-DOCSTRINGSUse reStructuredText format for docstrings. Docstring format, coverage, layout.py4xx/py40x-docstrings.xml
PY401-DOCSTRING-COVERAGEDocument all public functions, classes, and modules. Docstring format, coverage, layout.py4xx/py40x-docstrings.xml
PY402-MODULE-DOCSTRINGSEvery module has a docstring explaining its purpose. Docstring format, coverage, layout.py4xx/py40x-docstrings.xml
PY403-ONE-LINE-SUMMARYFirst line of docstring is a brief summary, then a blank line, then details. Docstring format, coverage, layout.py4xx/py40x-docstrings.xml
PY404-TYPE-HINTS-AS-DOCSType hints document expected types; don’t repeat in docstrings. Docstring format, coverage, layout.py4xx/py40x-docstrings.xml
PY405-EXAMPLES-IN-DOCSTRINGSInclude usage examples in docstrings for complex functions. Docstring format, coverage, layout.py4xx/py40x-docstrings.xml
PY406-LINE-LENGTHMax 88 characters per line (Black standard). Docstring format, coverage, layout.py4xx/py40x-docstrings.xml
PY407-BLANK-LINESTwo blank lines between top-level definitions, one within classes. Docstring format, coverage, layout.py4xx/py40x-docstrings.xml
PY408-INDENTATION4 spaces per indentation level. Docstring format, coverage, layout.py4xx/py40x-docstrings.xml
PY409-CONTRACT-BASED-PRIVACYUse underscore prefix only for internal implementation details. Attributes that represent observable state or configuration should be public with a read-only contract. Docstring format, coverage, layout.py4xx/py40x-docstrings.xml

PY41x — Formatting and Strings

Section titled “PY41x — Formatting and Strings”

Quotes, f-strings, concatenation.

RuleSummarySource
PY410-DOUBLE-UNDERSCOREAvoid __name__ (dunder) unless implementing protocols. Quotes, f-strings, concatenation.py4xx/py41x-formatting-and-strings.xml
PY411-STRING-QUOTESConsistent quote style. Double quotes " preferred. Quotes, f-strings, concatenation.py4xx/py41x-formatting-and-strings.xml
PY412-F-STRINGSUse f-strings for formatting, not % or .format(). Quotes, f-strings, concatenation.py4xx/py41x-formatting-and-strings.xml
PY413-STRING-CONCATENATIONUse f-strings or str.join(), not + in loops. Quotes, f-strings, concatenation.py4xx/py41x-formatting-and-strings.xml

PY5xx — Error Handling

Section titled “PY5xx — Error Handling”

Exception strategy and messages.

PY50x — Exception Types and Boundaries

Section titled “PY50x — Exception Types and Boundaries”

Specific exceptions, hierarchy, context.

RuleSummarySource
PY500-SPECIFIC-EXCEPTIONSCatch specific exceptions, not bare except:. Specific exceptions, hierarchy, context.py5xx/py50x-exception-types.xml
PY501-EXCEPTION-HIERARCHYCreate exception hierarchy for your domain. Specific exceptions, hierarchy, context.py5xx/py50x-exception-types.xml
PY502-RAISE-FROMUse raise ... from ... to preserve exception chain. Specific exceptions, hierarchy, context.py5xx/py50x-exception-types.xml
PY503-CONTEXT-MANAGERSUse context managers (with) for resource management. Specific exceptions, hierarchy, context.py5xx/py50x-exception-types.xml
PY504-SUPPRESS-EXCEPTIONSUse contextlib.suppress() for expected exceptions to ignore. Specific exceptions, hierarchy, context.py5xx/py50x-exception-types.xml
PY505-FINALLY-CLEANUPUse finally for cleanup that must happen regardless of exceptions. Specific exceptions, hierarchy, context.py5xx/py50x-exception-types.xml
PY506-ELSE-CLAUSEUse else in try/except for code that runs only if no exception. Specific exceptions, hierarchy, context.py5xx/py50x-exception-types.xml
PY507-CUSTOM-EXCEPTIONSDefine custom exceptions for your domain errors. Specific exceptions, hierarchy, context.py5xx/py50x-exception-types.xml
PY508-EXCEPTION-ARGSPass useful information to exceptions. Specific exceptions, hierarchy, context.py5xx/py50x-exception-types.xml
PY509-DONT-CATCH-ALLDon’t catch BaseException or Exception at top level unless re-raising. Specific exceptions, hierarchy, context.py5xx/py50x-exception-types.xml

PY51x — Control Flow and Messages

Section titled “PY51x — Control Flow and Messages”

Logging, assertions, validation, messages.

RuleSummarySource
PY510-LOGGING-EXCEPTIONSLog exceptions with context before re-raising or handling. Logging, assertions, validation, messages.py5xx/py51x-control-flow-and-messages.xml
PY511-ASSERT-FOR-BUGSUse assertions for impossible conditions (bugs), exceptions for expected errors. Logging, assertions, validation, messages.py5xx/py51x-control-flow-and-messages.xml
PY512-VALIDATION-EXCEPTIONSValidate inputs and raise exceptions early. Logging, assertions, validation, messages.py5xx/py51x-control-flow-and-messages.xml
PY513-ERROR-MESSAGESError messages should be clear, actionable, include context. Logging, assertions, validation, messages.py5xx/py51x-control-flow-and-messages.xml
PY514-EXITSTACKUse ExitStack for dynamic number of context managers. Logging, assertions, validation, messages.py5xx/py51x-control-flow-and-messages.xml

PY6xx — Performance & Best Practices

Section titled “PY6xx — Performance & Best Practices”

Profiling and optimization guidance.

PY60x — Profiling and Structure

Section titled “PY60x — Profiling and Structure”

Measure first, generators, caching, layout.

RuleSummarySource
PY600-PREMATURE-OPTIMIZATIONDon’t optimize until you measure. Clarity first. Measure first, generators, caching, layout.py6xx/py60x-profiling-and-structure/py600-premature-optimization.xml
PY601-PROFILE-FIRSTUse profiler (cProfile, line_profiler) to find actual bottlenecks. Measure first, generators, caching, layout.py6xx/py60x-profiling-and-structure/py601-profile-first.xml
PY602-GENERATOR-VS-LISTFavor generators over lists by default. Use generator expressions for large datasets to save memory and enable lazy evaluation. Only materialize to lists when you need random access, length, or multiple iterations. Measure first, generators, caching, layout.py6xx/py60x-profiling-and-structure/py602-generator-vs-list.xml
PY603-DICT-LOOKUPSUse dict lookups instead of long if/elif chains. Measure first, generators, caching, layout.py6xx/py60x-profiling-and-structure/py603-dict-lookups.xml
PY604-CACHINGCache expensive computations with @lru_cache or @cache. Measure first, generators, caching, layout.py6xx/py60x-profiling-and-structure/py604-caching.xml
PY605-STRING-PERFORMANCEUse str.join() for concatenating many strings. Measure first, generators, caching, layout.py6xx/py60x-profiling-and-structure/py605-string-performance.xml
PY606-SLOTSUse __slots__ for memory optimization in classes with many instances. Measure first, generators, caching, layout.py6xx/py60x-profiling-and-structure/py606-slots.xml
PY607-LOCAL-LOOKUPSLocal variables faster than global/attribute lookups. Measure first, generators, caching, layout.py6xx/py60x-profiling-and-structure/py607-local-lookups.xml
PY608-EARLY-RETURNReturn early from functions to reduce nesting. Measure first, generators, caching, layout.py6xx/py60x-profiling-and-structure/py608-early-return.xml
PY609-AVOID-DOTSIn hot loops, minimize attribute access. Measure first, generators, caching, layout.py6xx/py60x-profiling-and-structure/py609-avoid-dots.xml

PY61x — Micro-optimizations

Section titled “PY61x — Micro-optimizations”

Built-ins, regex, sets, lazy imports.

RuleSummarySource
PY610-BUILT-INSUse built-in functions (C-optimized) when possible. Built-ins, regex, sets, lazy imports.py6xx/py61x-micro-optimizations.xml
PY611-NUMPY-PANDASFor numerical data, use numpy/pandas (vectorized operations). Built-ins, regex, sets, lazy imports.py6xx/py61x-micro-optimizations.xml
PY612-COMPILE-REGEXCompile regexes used repeatedly. Built-ins, regex, sets, lazy imports.py6xx/py61x-micro-optimizations.xml
PY613-SET-MEMBERSHIPUse sets for membership testing (in), not lists. Built-ins, regex, sets, lazy imports.py6xx/py61x-micro-optimizations.xml
PY614-LAZY-IMPORTSImport expensive modules only when needed. Built-ins, regex, sets, lazy imports.py6xx/py61x-micro-optimizations.xml

Anti-Patterns

Section titled “Anti-Patterns”

Patterns to avoid.

PZ1xx — Type Safety Violations

Section titled “PZ1xx — Type Safety Violations”

Type misuse and variance issues.

PZ10x — Missing or Weak Types

Section titled “PZ10x — Missing or Weak Types”

Any overuse, missing hints, weak contracts.

RuleSummarySource
PZ100-OVERUSE-ANYUsing Any everywhere instead of proper types. Any overuse, missing hints, weak contracts.pz1xx/pz10x-missing-or-weak-types.xml
PZ101-MISSING-RETURN-TYPEFunctions without return type annotations. Any overuse, missing hints, weak contracts.pz1xx/pz10x-missing-or-weak-types.xml
PZ102-IGNORING-TYPE-ERRORSUsing # type: ignore comments instead of fixing root causes. Any overuse, missing hints, weak contracts.pz1xx/pz10x-missing-or-weak-types.xml
PZ103-TYPE-CASTING-WITHOUT-VALIDATIONcast(Type, value) without runtime check. Any overuse, missing hints, weak contracts.pz1xx/pz10x-missing-or-weak-types.xml
PZ104-IMPLICIT-OPTIONALdef func(x: int = None) without &#124; None. Any overuse, missing hints, weak contracts.pz1xx/pz10x-missing-or-weak-types.xml
PZ105-MIXED-TYPES-COLLECTIONSList[Union[int, str, dict]] grab bag. Any overuse, missing hints, weak contracts.pz1xx/pz10x-missing-or-weak-types.xml
PZ106-UNTYPED-DICTSDict[str, Any] for structured data. Any overuse, missing hints, weak contracts.pz1xx/pz10x-missing-or-weak-types.xml
PZ107-MISSING-PARAMETER-TYPESFunction parameters without type hints. Any overuse, missing hints, weak contracts.pz1xx/pz10x-missing-or-weak-types.xml
PZ108-RETURN-MULTIPLE-TYPESReturning different types without union. Any overuse, missing hints, weak contracts.pz1xx/pz10x-missing-or-weak-types.xml
PZ109-MUTABLE-DEFAULTSdef func(items=[]) or def func(config={}). Any overuse, missing hints, weak contracts.pz1xx/pz10x-missing-or-weak-types.xml

PZ11x — Type Violations

Section titled “PZ11x — Type Violations”

Variance, runtime misuse, dataclass misuse.

RuleSummarySource
PZ110-MISSING-PROTOCOL-METHODSClass claims to implement protocol but missing methods. Variance, runtime misuse, dataclass misuse.pz1xx/pz11x-type-violations/pz110-missing-protocol-methods.xml
PZ111-WRONG-VARIANCECovariant/contravariant used incorrectly. Variance, runtime misuse, dataclass misuse.pz1xx/pz11x-type-violations/pz111-wrong-variance.xml
PZ112-ISINSTANCE-ON-TYPINGisinstance(x, List[int]) doesn’t work. Variance, runtime misuse, dataclass misuse.pz1xx/pz11x-type-violations/pz112-isinstance-on-typing.xml
PZ113-MIXED-SYNC-ASYNCConfusing sync/async in type hints. Variance, runtime misuse, dataclass misuse.pz1xx/pz11x-type-violations/pz113-mixed-sync-async.xml
PZ114-UNION-NONE-LASTNone &#124; int instead of int &#124; None. Variance, runtime misuse, dataclass misuse.pz1xx/pz11x-type-violations/pz114-union-none-last.xml
PZ115-DATACLASS-WITH-BEHAVIORUsing @dataclass decorator for classes with complex behavior, business logic, or methods beyond simple accessors. Variance, runtime misuse, dataclass misuse.pz1xx/pz11x-type-violations/pz115-dataclass-with-behavior.xml

PZ2xx — Mutability Problems

Section titled “PZ2xx — Mutability Problems”

Shared state and mutation pitfalls.

PZ20x — Shared State Issues

Section titled “PZ20x — Shared State Issues”

Globals, shared mutable state, caching.

RuleSummarySource
PZ200-MUTATING-ARGUMENTSFunction modifies its arguments. Globals, shared mutable state, caching.pz2xx/pz20x-shared-state.xml
PZ201-GLOBAL-STATEFunctions read/write global variables. Globals, shared mutable state, caching.pz2xx/pz20x-shared-state.xml
PZ202-MUTABLE-CLASS-ATTRIBUTESMutable class variable shared across instances. Globals, shared mutable state, caching.pz2xx/pz20x-shared-state.xml
PZ203-SHARED-MUTABLE-REFSMultiple references to same mutable object. Globals, shared mutable state, caching.pz2xx/pz20x-shared-state.xml
PZ204-MUTATING-DURING-ITERATIONModifying collection while iterating. Globals, shared mutable state, caching.pz2xx/pz20x-shared-state.xml
PZ205-PROPERTY-WITH-SIDE-EFFECTSProperty getter that modifies state or does I/O. Globals, shared mutable state, caching.pz2xx/pz20x-shared-state.xml
PZ206-MUTABLE-RETURN-INTERNALReturning mutable internal state. Globals, shared mutable state, caching.pz2xx/pz20x-shared-state.xml
PZ207-MODIFYING-CONSTANTSChanging values marked as constants. Globals, shared mutable state, caching.pz2xx/pz20x-shared-state.xml
PZ208-SINGLETON-MUTABLE-STATESingleton with mutable state. Globals, shared mutable state, caching.pz2xx/pz20x-shared-state.xml
PZ209-CACHING-MUTABLECaching mutable objects. Globals, shared mutable state, caching.pz2xx/pz20x-shared-state.xml

PZ21x — Mutation Hazards

Section titled “PZ21x — Mutation Hazards”

In-place ops, loop mutation, frozen violations.

RuleSummarySource
PZ210-MUTATING-LOOP-VARIABLEModifying loop variable inside loop. In-place ops, loop mutation, frozen violations.pz2xx/pz21x-mutation-hazards.xml
PZ211-DEFAULT-DICT-MUTATIONMutating default dict values. In-place ops, loop mutation, frozen violations.pz2xx/pz21x-mutation-hazards.xml
PZ212-IN-PLACE-OPERATIONSlist.sort() instead of sorted(list). In-place ops, loop mutation, frozen violations.pz2xx/pz21x-mutation-hazards.xml
PZ213-FROZEN-VIOLATIONWorking around frozen dataclass. In-place ops, loop mutation, frozen violations.pz2xx/pz21x-mutation-hazards.xml
PZ214-MUTABLE-DATACLASSDataclass without frozen=True when should be immutable. In-place ops, loop mutation, frozen violations.pz2xx/pz21x-mutation-hazards.xml

py100 use type hints

PY100-USE-TYPE-HINTS

Section titled “PY100-USE-TYPE-HINTS”

Always use type hints for function parameters, return values, and class attributes.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Type hints improve code clarity, enable static analysis, catch bugs early, and serve as documentation.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

mypy strict mode requires type hints. ruff rule ANN enforces annotations.

Examples

Section titled “Examples”

Good Example 1

Section titled “Good Example 1”

Bad Example 1

Section titled “Bad Example 1”

Related Rules

Section titled “Related Rules”
  • PY101-PRECISE-TYPES
  • PZ107-MISSING-PARAMETER-TYPES

View raw XML source

py101 precise types

PY101-PRECISE-TYPES

Section titled “PY101-PRECISE-TYPES”

Use the most specific type possible. Prefer Sequence[str] over list, Mapping[str, int] over dict.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Precise types communicate intent, enable better type checking, and document constraints.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

mypy detects type mismatches. Use from collections.abc import Sequence, Mapping, Iterable.

Examples

Section titled “Examples”

Good Example 1

Section titled “Good Example 1”

Bad Example 1

Section titled “Bad Example 1”

Good Example 2

Section titled “Good Example 2”

Bad Example 2

Section titled “Bad Example 2”

Related Rules

Section titled “Related Rules”
  • PY102-USE-PROTOCOLS
  • PY106-COLLECTION-TYPES

View raw XML source

py102 use protocols

PY102-USE-PROTOCOLS

Section titled “PY102-USE-PROTOCOLS”

Prefer Protocol over ABC for structural subtyping. Use ABC only when inheritance is semantically important.

Exceptions

Section titled “Exceptions”

Use ABC when you need shared implementation via inheritance (template method pattern), or when semantic inheritance is important for your domain.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Protocols enable duck typing with type safety. They’re more flexible than ABCs and don’t require explicit inheritance.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

mypy supports Protocol from typing. Python 3.8+.

Examples

Section titled “Examples”

Good Example 1

Section titled “Good Example 1”

Good Example 2

Section titled “Good Example 2”

Bad Example 1

Section titled “Bad Example 1”

Related Rules

Section titled “Related Rules”
  • Y101-INTERFACE-SEGREGATION
  • Y220-STRATEGY-PATTERN

View raw XML source

py103 use generics

PY103-USE-GENERICS

Section titled “PY103-USE-GENERICS”

Use generic types for reusable components that work with multiple types. Prefer Python 3.12+ native syntax with automatic variance inference.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Generics enable type-safe reuse without duplication. They preserve type information through transformations. Python 3.12+ syntax is cleaner and automatically infers variance from usage, eliminating manual TypeVar declarations.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

Python 3.12+ required. mypy 1.7+ supports this syntax. For older Python versions, use TypeVar + Generic.

Examples

Section titled “Examples”

Good Example 1

Section titled “Good Example 1”

Good Example 2

Section titled “Good Example 2”

Bad Example 1

Section titled “Bad Example 1”

Related Rules

Section titled “Related Rules”
  • PY100-USE-TYPE-HINTS
  • PY101-PRECISE-TYPES
  • PY128-NO-EXPORT-TYPEVARS

Notes

Section titled “Notes”
  • Benefits of Python 3.12+ syntax: - Cleaner code: No separate TypeVar declarations - Automatic variance: Python infers covariance/contravariance from usage - Better readability: Generic parameters visible in class/function signature - Fewer imports: No need to import TypeVar, Generic

View raw XML source

py104 type aliases

PY104-TYPE-ALIASES

Section titled “PY104-TYPE-ALIASES”

Use type aliases for complex types. Use TypeAlias annotation for clarity.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Type aliases improve readability, reduce duplication, and centralize type definitions.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

Use TypeAlias from typing (Python 3.10+) or typing_extensions.

Examples

Section titled “Good Example 1”

Bad Example 1

Section titled “Bad Example 1”

Related Rules

Section titled “Related Rules”
  • PY101-PRECISE-TYPES
  • PY105-NEWTYPE

View raw XML source

py105 newtype

PY105-NEWTYPE

Section titled “PY105-NEWTYPE”

Use NewType to create distinct types from primitives when semantic distinction is important.

Exceptions

Section titled “Exceptions”

Don’t overuse. Only create NewTypes when semantic confusion is a real risk.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

NewType prevents mixing semantically different values of the same primitive type (e.g., user_id vs transaction_id).

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

mypy enforces NewType distinctions.

Examples

Section titled “Examples”

Good Example 1

Section titled “Good Example 1”

Bad Example 1

Section titled “Bad Example 1”

Related Rules

Section titled “Related Rules”
  • PY100-USE-TYPE-HINTS
  • PY104-TYPE-ALIASES

View raw XML source

py106 collection types

PY106-COLLECTION-TYPES

Section titled “PY106-COLLECTION-TYPES”

Use appropriate collection types from collections.abc: Sequence, Mapping, Iterable, Iterator, Collection.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Abstract collection types are more flexible than concrete types, enabling broader compatibility.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

Import from collections.abc (not typing). mypy checks compatibility.

Examples

Section titled “Examples”

Good Example 1

Section titled “Good Example 1”

Good Example 2

Section titled “Good Example 2”

Bad Example 1

Section titled “Bad Example 1”

Related Rules

Section titled “Related Rules”
  • PY101-PRECISE-TYPES
  • PY300-IMMUTABILITY

Notes

Section titled “Notes”
  • Collection Types: - Iterable[T]: Can be iterated (for loop), one pass - Sequence[T]: Ordered, indexed, can iterate multiple times (list, tuple) - Mapping[K, V]: Key-value pairs, read-only (dict, but immutable view) - MutableMapping[K, V]: Mutable key-value pairs - Collection[T]: Has length, contains checks, iterable

View raw XML source

py107 literal types

PY107-LITERAL-TYPES

Section titled “PY107-LITERAL-TYPES”

Use Literal for values that must be specific constants.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Literal types provide compile-time checking for magic strings/numbers, preventing typos.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

Literal from typing. mypy checks exhaustiveness.

Bad Example 1

Related Rules

Section titled “Related Rules”
  • PY101-PRECISE-TYPES
  • PY108-FINAL

View raw XML source

py108 final

PY108-FINAL

Section titled “PY108-FINAL”

Use Final for constants that should never be reassigned.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Final prevents accidental modification of constants, documents immutability intent.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

Final from typing. mypy enforces finality.

Examples

Section titled “Examples”

Good Example 1

Section titled “Good Example 1”

Good Example 2

Section titled “Good Example 2”

Bad Example 1

Section titled “Bad Example 1”

Related Rules

Section titled “Related Rules”
  • PY107-LITERAL-TYPES
  • PY300-IMMUTABILITY

View raw XML source

py109 avoid any

PY109-AVOID-ANY

Section titled “PY109-AVOID-ANY”

Avoid Any type unless absolutely necessary. Use generics, unions, or protocols instead.

Exceptions

Section titled “Exceptions”

Use Any when:

  1. Interfacing with truly dynamic code (e.g., JSON parsing before validation)
  2. Gradual typing migration (temporary, document plan to remove)
  3. Type is genuinely unknowable (rare)

Always document why Any is needed.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Any disables type checking, defeating the purpose of type hints. It’s a type system escape hatch.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

mypy —strict warns on implicit Any. ruff ANN401 warns on explicit Any (currently ignored in this project).

Examples

Section titled “Examples”

Good Example 1

Bad Example 1

Section titled “Bad Example 1”

Good Example 2

Bad Example 2

Section titled “Bad Example 2”

  • PY103-USE-GENERICS
  • PZ100-OVERUSE-ANY

py110 union types

PY110-UNION-TYPES

Section titled “PY110-UNION-TYPES”

Use modern union syntax X | Y (Python 3.10+) instead of Union[X, Y] or Optional[X].

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Cleaner syntax, more readable, aligns with PEP 604.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

Python 3.10+ required. mypy supports both syntaxes.

Section titled “Good Example 1”

Bad Example 1

Section titled “Bad Example 1”

Related Rules

Section titled “Related Rules”
  • PY100-USE-TYPE-HINTS
  • PY101-PRECISE-TYPES

View raw XML source

py111 type narrowing

PY111-TYPE-NARROWING

Section titled “PY111-TYPE-NARROWING”

Use type narrowing with isinstance(), is None checks, and TypeGuard for complex narrowing.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Type narrowing helps mypy understand conditional type refinement, reducing false positives.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

mypy performs automatic narrowing for isinstance, is None. TypeGuard from typing for custom guards.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”

Related Rules

Section titled “Related Rules”
  • PY100-USE-TYPE-HINTS
  • PY110-UNION-TYPES

View raw XML source

py112 dataclass types

PY112-DATACLASS-TYPES

Section titled “PY112-DATACLASS-TYPES”

Use dataclasses with full type hints for data containers.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Dataclasses reduce boilerplate, integrate with type checking, and provide immutability options.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

Dataclasses from dataclasses module (Python 3.7+). mypy checks field types.

  • PY100-USE-TYPE-HINTS
  • PY300-IMMUTABILITY
  • PY301-FROZEN-DATACLASSES

View raw XML source

py113 callable types

PY113-CALLABLE-TYPES

Section titled “PY113-CALLABLE-TYPES”

Use Callable type hint for functions passed as parameters.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Documents expected function signature, enables type checking of callbacks.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

Callable from collections.abc. Syntax: Callable[[ArgTypes...], ReturnType].

Examples

Bad Example 1

py114 namedtuple types

PY114-NAMEDTUPLE-TYPES

Section titled “PY114-NAMEDTUPLE-TYPES”

Use NamedTuple with type hints for simple immutable data structures.

When To Use

Section titled “When To Use”
  • NamedTuple: Simple, immutable data with few fields
  • dataclass: More complex data, needs methods, optional mutability
  • Plain tuple: Only for very short-lived, obvious positional data

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

NamedTuples are immutable, lightweight, and have good performance. Better than plain tuples.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

NamedTuple from typing. mypy checks field types.

Examples

  • PY300-IMMUTABILITY

py115 return type hints

PY115-RETURN-TYPE-HINTS

Section titled “PY115-RETURN-TYPE-HINTS”

Always specify return types, even for functions returning None.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Explicit return types document intent, catch missing returns, and enable better type inference.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

mypy —strict requires return type annotations. ruff ANN201/ANN202 enforce.

py116 class var

PY116-CLASS-VAR

Section titled “PY116-CLASS-VAR”

Use ClassVar to annotate class variables (not instance variables).

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Distinguishes class-level from instance-level attributes, prevents mypy confusion.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

ClassVar from typing. mypy enforces correct usage.

py117 self type

PY117-SELF-TYPE

Section titled “PY117-SELF-TYPE”

Use Self type for methods returning the instance (method chaining, factory methods).

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Self correctly handles inheritance, unlike hardcoded class names.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

Self from typing (Python 3.11+) or typing_extensions.

py118 strict mode

PY118-STRICT-MODE

Section titled “PY118-STRICT-MODE”

Enable mypy strict mode in your project configuration.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Strict mode catches more errors, enforces best practices, ensures consistency.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

mypy --strict flag or strict = true in config.

Related Rules

Section titled “Related Rules”
  • PY100-USE-TYPE-HINTS
  • PY109-AVOID-ANY

Notes

Section titled “Notes”
  • Benefits: - Forces comprehensive type coverage - Catches bugs early - Improves code documentation - Enables confident refactoring
  • Configuration: toml # pyproject.toml [tool.mypy] python_version = "3.13" strict = true warn_return_any = true warn_unused_configs = true
  • What strict mode enables: - --disallow-untyped-defs: All functions must have type hints - --disallow-any-unimported: Can’t use Any from untyped imports - --warn-return-any: Warns when returning Any - --warn-redundant-casts: Catches unnecessary casts - --no-implicit-optional: x: int = None is an error - And more…

View raw XML source

py119 type comments

PY119-TYPE-COMMENTS

Section titled “PY119-TYPE-COMMENTS”

Avoid type comments. Use modern annotation syntax instead.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Type comments are legacy syntax for Python <3.6. Modern annotations are clearer.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

Modern annotations work in Python 3.6+. Use them exclusively.

Section titled “Examples”

Good Example 1

Section titled “Good Example 1”

Bad Example 1

Section titled “Related Rules”

py120 type checking

PY120-TYPE-CHECKING

Section titled “PY120-TYPE-CHECKING”

Use if TYPE_CHECKING: for import-only type hints to avoid circular imports.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

TYPE_CHECKING is False at runtime, allowing imports only needed for type checking.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

TYPE_CHECKING from typing. mypy evaluates as True.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”

Section titled “Related Rules”
  • PY100-USE-TYPE-HINTS

py121 forward refs

PY121-FORWARD-REFS

Section titled “PY121-FORWARD-REFS”

Use forward references (string literals) for types not yet defined or circular dependencies.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Forward refs allow referencing types before they’re defined.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

String literals or from __future__ import annotations.

Good Example

Section titled “Good Example”

Section titled “Related Rules”
  • PY100-USE-TYPE-HINTS
  • PY120-TYPE-CHECKING

py122 overload

PY122-OVERLOAD

Section titled “PY122-OVERLOAD”

Use @overload for functions with different signatures based on argument types.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Overloads provide precise type information for different call patterns.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

@overload from typing. mypy checks signature compatibility.

py123 paramspec

PY123-PARAMSPEC

Section titled “PY123-PARAMSPEC”

Use ParamSpec to preserve function signatures in decorators.

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

ParamSpec ensures decorators don’t lose parameter type information.

Tool Support

ParamSpec from typing (Python 3.10+) or typing_extensions.

py124 concatenate

PY124-CONCATENATE

Section titled “PY124-CONCATENATE”

Use Concatenate to add parameters to callables (useful for method decorators).

Rationale

Section titled “Rationale”

Concatenate allows prepending parameters to Callable types, enabling precise typing of method decorators.

Tool Support

Section titled “Tool Support”

Concatenate from typing (Python 3.10+) or typing_extensions.
